In practice, although the International Classification of Diseases (ICD) is known worldwide, unfortunately what is still perceived is many doctors labeling all people who say they hear voices or see spirits as psychotic and treating them with heavy medications for the rest of their lives.

The vast majority of patients, labeled by psychiatrists as “psychotic” for hearing voices (clairaudience) or seeing spirits (clairvoyance), are, in fact, mediums with mediumistic imbalance and not with a mental, psychiatric imbalance. Therefore, spiritual obsession as an ailment of the soul deserves to be studied seriously and in depth so that we can improve the quality of life of these mediums.
a-verdadeira-e-a-falsa-rebeldia-jovem: It is important that young people know that there are two ways to be rebellious: one is to rebel against society, against your father, against your mother, against your family, against a portion of politicians, against corruption, against social injustices, against hunger, against misery, against violence, in a negative way, or to rebel against all of this in a positive way. How are both of these ways?

The way that should preferentially be chosen by young people, if they were not daily, mentally washed by the created image of “Being young is…”, would be positive rebellion, that is, seeking to be different, trying to be better, trying to set an example for adults, that is, if squares adults smoke, not to smoke, if squares adults drink, not to drink, if squares adults are complacent, not to be complacent, if some politicians are corrupt, if they steal, if they deceive, not to be corrupt, not to steal, not to deceive, if social injustice, hunger, misery, violence in Brazil and in the world bother you, join NGOs, Associations, Entities, that fight against this, so that one day no one else goes hungry, so that no one else lives in misery, so that violence ends. This is positive rebellion.

But, unfortunately, a certain portion of young people rebels in a negative way, that is, they rebel against what is wrong by acting wrong, and how is that? Smoking, drinking, doing drugs, doing badly in high school, badly in college, living at night, or shutting themselves inside, hurting themselves, getting depressed, getting angry, in short, a way to rebel that leads nowhere, only leads to their own destruction, in a misguided channeling of their young strength, of their adolescent indignation, which should be positive but is negative.

To those, we extend our congratulations, for they will change the world; to these, we offer advice: be different from what you disapprove of, do not be the same, for you are keeping the world exactly as it is or even making it worse. Do not be impressed by the American model massively disseminated by the media, for this is prefabricated and only wants you to be consumers, docile sheep, ready to acquire and be what they decree is fashionable, what is “young,” and do not be impressed by or imitate the European rebellious model, for it is decadent and at times even ridiculous in a tropical country like ours, full of sun, blue sky, and nature, and not cold, gray, and obscure.

There are two ways for a young person to want to be independent: one is by thinking they are independent, but, in reality, being a prisoner of the media message that convinced them that being an independent young person is to smoke, drink, “enjoy life,” be crazy, a bit wild, which is the American and European style of being young.

The other way is to truly be independent, not following what commercial messages incentivize, interested in transforming young people into mere consumers, into a source of income, and then being free from these capitalist, consumerist, alienating messages, and being a truly independent young person, who does not obey what they say, what young people “should do,” how they “should be,” that you “should smoke,” that you “should drink,” that you “should enjoy” nights, weekends, holidays.

To be independent is to control oneself, but for this, young people must develop discernment, a critical view of what enters their eyes and ears, whether it is a positive or negative message, whether it aims to encourage them to use their youthful strength, their indignation, positively, to improve the world, or to “enjoy life,” which they tell them is only one, they tell them that youth passes quickly, that they must do everything they can, before – oh the horror! – becoming an adult and, even worse, growing old!

In the East, it is a source of pride to grow old; people are sought out by the younger generation for advice and guidance. But here in the West, growing old is a horror, and sometimes even a kind of shame that so many adults, their minds dominated by these materialistic ideas, try to deceive themselves, but this is impossible. The difference between a 20-year-old and a 60-year-old is only 40 years, and that, considering the age of our Spirit, which is several hundred thousand years old, is almost nothing…

Young people like to break rules, but which rules should be broken? One of the rules that young people should break is the rule created by some segments of industry and commerce, that “Being young is being wild”—this should definitely be broken! Some youth radio programs and some programs and films on television and websites on the internet encourage this conception; it should be completely broken. Young people of Brazil: break this rule!

But a rule that should never be broken is the moral rule that says young people should strive to use their energy and youthful strength to improve the world, to end the habit of smoking, drinking, and using other drugs, and to know how to use their time to collaborate in social, spiritual, humanitarian organizations, to help improve the world. This rule should be immediately adopted! Instead of going to the square to smoke a joint, instead of going to the bar to drink beer and chat, instead of spending hours and hours in front of the television watching

programs that activate our lower chakras or on the computer “chatting” or playing or passing the time, rebel against that and look for an institution where you can join people who work to help the poor, the abandoned, needy and sick brothers and sisters. That is a great rebellion.

Another rule that should be broken is the rule that says young people should study and work in anything that makes money, have a good job where they don't have to try too hard, and not care what they produce or sell, whether it is good and healthy or bad and harmful to people. This rule must be broken! A good way to rebel against this rule is to only work for companies that aim to help the world improve, that produce healthy and clean products, that sell good things to people. This is a truly youthful rebellion.

A rule that should not be broken is that young people should study, be excellent students, to become excellent professionals and work in companies, in governmental bodies, that are characterized by having an activity that helps to end social injustice, hunger, misery, and violence in Brazil and in the world. This rule should not be broken.

Changing the world for the better should be the main activity and goal of young people. For this, young people need to understand what makes the world worse, what keeps it as it is, and what can improve it, so that one day it becomes what we, the utopians, want. For example, a young person is an idealist and wants to improve the world, and goes to work in a cigarette or alcoholic beverage factory, or in a distributor of these drugs, or in some place where sales are encouraged. Where did their ideal go? They are serving two Masters, Light and Shadow. What will most likely happen is that they will kill their dream and become a cynical and hypocritical adult; they will be an adult who will hide behind a sad smile, someone who killed their youthful ideal, who is doing what they know is not good for the world, who works in a place that has no social concern to be useful to people, but only aims to sell and make money.

Another example: a young person wants to improve the world and goes to work for a company that manufactures, distributes, or sells superfluous products, which only encourage consumerism, superficiality, and frivolity in other young people. Where is their will to improve the world? Only in their head, but not in their practice, and then it's no use.
* Text extracted from the book “How to evolve spiritually in a world of drugs” and the website “www.visibilidadezero.com.br”
freud-alem-da-vida: Message received from Sigmund Freud by the German medium, Eva Herrmann
Extracted from the book published by Editora Otto Reichl Verlag, with the title “From the other side”
__SUB__Report from the medium Eva Herrmann

In 1973 I addressed a question to Sigmund (spirit), assuming that only he could answer it accurately. The subject today is irrelevant. But Freud, who soon presented himself, seemed to have little interest in talking to me and, to my dismay, was unfriendly. Freud, however, made it unequivocally clear to me that he considered my communication frivolous and therefore wished to be left in peace in the future. But, the next day, he appeared again and apologized, stating that he had informed himself about me and my communications and had changed his mind. In the course of our conversation, he expressed the desire to make a confession. I hesitated, as I was, along with my assistants, in the middle of another work, and moreover, I considered it doubtful whether such a confession would deserve any credibility. I also knew from experience what affliction resulted from hearing and noting such self-accusations. But Freud insisted and so I received his “general confession”. I present it here and hope that it can be received by those interested in Freud and psychic matters, with an open mind.
Eva Herrmann
__SUB__Sigmund Freud’s Confession through the medium Eva Herrmann
“I, Sigmund Freud, will transmit a confession to Eva Herrmann, which for now should not be made public to avoid involving people close to me in a matter that may be unpleasant for them.

I dictated this general confession to Eva Herrmann because I hoped to gain greater clarity about my life and to free myself from ties to the past. These ties are a great burden for me, as they incessantly remind me of the mistakes I made, both in my private life and in my life's work. I would like to clarify both, but first I wanted to talk about personal mistakes, as they are what keep me here in the characteristic circles of spirits not yet purified.

This takes me right into the middle of the metaphysical realm, for with the above sentence, I establish a series of premises that, on the one hand, are irreconcilable with the opinions I formerly held and, on the other, far exceed them. I mention this as an introduction, as it may surprise many who are accustomed to reading my books, when they perceive the words and the meaning of my confession. However, this new approach is the result of being in this world, of which I had no knowledge and which now presents itself before my eyes in an irrefutable way. All internal resistance to not accepting it leads to nothing. It exists, inexorable for those who have been extradited here, and blessed for those who belong to it by tendency or development: to those of religious tendency or to the simply good.

As I had no religious tendencies, nor characteristics that collectively result in the truly good man, I now find myself on a plane that, to use common parlance, belongs to darkness or “Hell”. Basically, this Hell houses those who, by their spiritual conditions, are unhappy or evil. The former are usually allowed to elevate their condition through the necessary self-will: for the evil, this depends on whether they feel good here or not. This statement may sound surprising, but I had to get used to the idea that evil exists by itself, and that many spirits surrendered partially or totally to it. If this does not align with currently accepted assumptions, it can only be regretted that most men insist on ignoring this fact which, unfortunately, belongs to the basic realities of life.

The view commonly held today, that evil acts are the result of a childhood in which the person was hurt – a view to which I greatly contributed – has demonstrated to me here as totally mistaken, as man is the result of much more than a more or less happy childhood. Man is the final sum of previous earthly existences, which often took place centuries before the current life. The events of childhood only constitute the resumption of the predominant path of each life. A path that is often basic for several successive lives, until the soul can free itself from an unhappiness that is its own. But it is not always a matter of unhappiness. Many souls have an inheritance, they continue from the point where they had to interrupt, in the past, the path to the improvement of one or another area of knowledge.

Genius! What is a genius? A soul that has passed through an infinitely large number of incarnations, directed towards a single goal, until they can finally be born as a Shakespeare, Mozart or Einstein, and thus find their fulfillment.

Talent belongs to the soul that is on the way to genius, on the assumption that it perseveres until the end. This perseverance is, however, not easy at all. The “evil” that we are now forced to consider a power equivalent to “good”, manifests itself in multiple ways. For example, as negligence, and negligence is the enemy of any ascent. A talented person may never become a genius if their soul becomes subservient to evil, in its most harmless mask – “indifference”.

The fact that I have perceived evil in all its disguises since I have been here is due to the recognition that two centers of power divide the dominion of the world between them and therefore fight each other: Good and Evil, that is, the constructive and the destructive, clarity and darkness, love and hate. Although the above seems indescribably simple, it is the truth and nothing more than that. But it ceases to be simple if all its intricate variants and ramifications are observed. And it turns out that the conception of the Universe, in the Middle Ages (perhaps on this single point), approaches what we are forced to recognize as irrefutable truth.

Already as a child, I fell prey to “evil” in the form described here. I hated my mother and my siblings, not as I later believed, because I was jealous of my father, but because a positioning provoked by a previous existence led me to it. What I call “positioning” here roughly corresponds to what Hindus call “karma”, that is, the transfer of fate-related facts to a new life. I had a clear intelligence, with intuition, and I was given the condition to influence the men of my time in such a way that I can rightly say that I substantially helped to establish the foundations of the spiritual landscape of that time. I was close to becoming the creator of a valid psychology, but the decisive condition was missing: I was not authorized to do so. I was only allowed to give the world a largely correct picture of the unconscious, but not to clarify the true nature of the soul. My karma hindered me in the form of imposed restrictions and allowed me to present the world with a “pseudo-truth”, since the world (or humanity on Earth) is also subject to a kind of karma by which it can only reach the correct understanding at the appointed time.

I hated my mother and my siblings, although I never admitted it during my life. They were strangers to me, each in their own way. I loved Martha, my wife, and I still love her: but she is on a much higher plane than mine and I can only reach her indirectly. As a result, I strive even harder to get out of my current condition. Since I had the magnificent experience of communicating with her through charitable entities that transmitted spoken communication, stage by stage, my longing to be by her side has intensified.

To return to my childhood, I hated everyone around me. I never mentioned it, both because of the ugliness of my feelings and because I had no explanation for it. I hated my mother despite a strong incestuous bond on my side, but both feelings ran in parallel, without disturbing each other. Later I explained this feeling by the supposed presence of an Oedipus complex, but today I see this picture in a completely different light. I hated my mother because, in a previous life, she did me a great injustice and I carried that fact with me, in the depths of my being.

But that I also desired her is related to the fact that the theory of the Oedipus complex is correct when there is a certain attraction between the sexes. One should not, however, conclude that all the rest of Sophocles' tragedy can or should be accepted as a whole. The above theory, which plays a major role in my work, I now reject as a flawed construct. The fact that millions of people accepted and still accept it today does not change the truth that destructive forces idealize this trick that led more than one generation to play the fool.
My colleagues, to whom this diagnosis seemed the correct answer to a given problem, as much as I was, were victims of a self-suggestion that takes hold of those who do not know a better solution.

I didn't just hate my mother, I hated all my siblings. For this I only have one explanation: the hatred for my mother, which was unjustifiable, colored my emotional life in such a way that the hatred overflowed to my siblings who, in themselves, were not at all hateful. They reacted normally, so that I found myself in a world full of hatred and antipathy. All this situation corresponded entirely to my karma, that is, my pre-established life that represented the result of previous incarnations. These incarnations of the human soul repeat themselves in periods of variable duration, the most advanced, at enormously long intervals.

My last incarnation took place during the Thirty Years' War (1618-1648) and I am beginning to remember that bad life. I had a high position, but it barely corresponded to my aptitudes, and I carried out my task with profound disdain for those around me, to whom I felt very superior. This positioning was linked to an unparalleled contempt for human needs. I thus returned to this world with the desire to help others, but, due to my karma, I only partially succeeded, because, despite my knowledge of the mechanism of the unconscious forming a basis for a valid psychology, the rest of my teaching is not only erroneous, and I confirm this with a heavy heart, but it is, in a way, absurd.

What I feel most deeply today, however, is that I have not used, since it was allowed to me, my time and work to discover the true bases of what makes man what he is, that is, to seek the destiny-forming events in reincarnation and in his past, which is lost in darkness, instead of looking for them in his childhood. To this determining background also belongs the influence of “forces from beyond”, whose existence I ignored. Only since my passing do I understand the enormous importance that these entities, good or bad, have for individual as well as collective life. This fact is of such paramount importance that I almost cannot find the words to present it in its complete significance.

Only since I, daily, witness that scene which shows, without exception, that humanity is a tool of the entities invisible to it, a divergence so fundamental from what most consider to be the normal course of events, do I begin to understand that all current concepts about the functioning of the human being do not even come close to reality. This influence is not arbitrary, but based on merit, corresponding, so to speak, to an optional kinship, which led me to the disturbing perception that the Universe is governed by “something” like a superhuman justice.

This superhuman justice presents itself to me as something truly divine, although not as a personified divinity. This justice, however, is only one aspect of that divinity that can be perceived as a radiant light penetrating the farthest depths of the Universe and blessing those who can partake in it. This, however, is not given to just anyone, since a large part of humanity is in such spiritual conditions that, as if covered by a hard crust of ignorance and wickedness, the divine light cannot penetrate them. Regarding this, I want to mention a magnitude, or dimension that, beyond height, width, and depth, indicates the degree of density, be it of a soul or of a sphere corresponding to it, a degree of density that is not tangible, but is an indication of the degree of spiritualization.

If a soul is in a state of spiritual-religious blindness, the opacity of such a soul prevents it from being permeated by a clarity that is the simultaneous expression of love, cognition, and absolute justice. In this context, I understand religiosity today as something very different from a blind observance of a ritual, or religious fanaticism. The aforementioned impermeability is also felt as distance from the source of light and is associated with an uneasiness proportional to this distance. In certain cases, this is denied when, for example, there is a complete detachment from the divine, but it is understandable to speak of an “above”, close to the light, and a “below”, far from it. As, moreover, numerous souls belong to the same degree of density, thus forming a collective, it is understandable that the various spheres are, based on very old traditions, called by symbolic designations of Heaven and Hell, and within these, by differentiations expressed by numbers. It is natural that, in relation to these expressions, one should not think of locations and, especially, not of anything that in any way resembles traditional images. To conclude, I would like to mention that the task to which we belong is not something external to us but, rather, the result of an internal condition, projected outward.

My soul is still in a state of relative density, for it is burdened with guilt yet to be compensated. In the world here, there can be no illusions about the condition of one's soul, as it is confronted with an objective and inexorable positioning. This positioning is the result of the fact that the soul can only exist in a sphere that is appropriate for it, and this sphere is entirely evident.

The guilt I speak of can only with difficulty be translated into words, for it is a continuity that began in my childhood and, so to speak, accompanied my growth. As I mentioned above, I hated my mother and my siblings. My father was kept at a distance by me. I didn't hate him, even though this would have perfectly fit my theory; he was just disagreeable to me, like most of my relatives. Today it's clear to me, but during my life on Earth this difficult relationship with those around me produced a deep resentment, which grew over time. I considered myself an original, eccentric person and... a genius. However, I didn't appreciate it when these designations were used in relation to me, as they embarrassed me because I couldn't respond to such a compliment in a polite way. I considered myself a misunderstood genius and, despite believing in my own theories, it was a pleasure for me to shock others with them. I wanted to take revenge on the world.

I was shy and withdrawn, not to say bitter. Even though I was eager to help individual patients (and nothing was too much for me to do so), I took deep satisfaction in showing the world a mirror in which everyone could perceive a distorted picture of themselves. Naturally, this was done with the utmost objectivity and reserve, as behooved a scientist. From my current perspective, there is no doubt that this attitude was accompanied by a true sadistic pleasure that I carefully guarded in the depths of my soul. This was especially easy, considering that I made a substantial contribution regarding the mechanism of the unconscious, which I consider entirely correct and always have.

But almost all other collaborations and embellishments were, as I now know, transmitted to me by “forces” who maliciously delighted in finding a serious man who would listen to them. The above collaborations showed man from a perspective with a unilateral sexual emphasis, which diverted the attention of an entire generation to concepts that did not correspond to reality, or that did not have universal scope, as I proclaimed. Seen subjectively, from the viewpoint of someone who has lost the scales before their eyes, there was a mistake on my part, favored, in a way, by the internal disposition of wanting to shock the world. Objectively, I was a victim of karma that referred to me and the world in general, thus allowing the “forces of darkness” to temporarily present man as completely different from what he is in reality.

I do not know how long it will take me, with the help of others, to rehabilitate man as a creation of God, whose sexuality, with the exception of pathological cases, constitutes an important part of his earthly life. This creature of God should once again be allowed to reach knowledge that today is either forgotten or continues to exist in a form that has rendered it unacceptable to any reasonably demanding man. But it is to be hoped that light will soon fall on this soulless darkness, in which phrases that only sound modern teem. Man will be repositioned as the being who must choose between “Heaven” and “Hell” (disregarding the unpleasant taste of these terms); as a being whose sexuality sometimes brings him problems (or brought them) when oppression, repressions or embellishment of an instinct were commonplace. The pendulum swings to the opposite position and man finally becomes the being who has much to learn and almost much to unlearn.

Clearly, penance has the property of threatening to suffocate me due to what the hatred, which I only now recognize as such, made me do. What does penance mean, a word with which we associate scourging, flagellation, and masochistic feelings, in the light of new knowledge? Penance is the method by which the soul purifies itself from a committed injustice, which clouds its own substance and prevents it from participating in the divine. One is unhappy and would like to change this. It is not possible to undo what has happened and so one tries, in one way or another, to compensate for the mistake made by something good that does not eliminate the injustice, but in a way compensates for it. My current work is this. It consists, as a first step, in the recognition and evaluation of the injustice committed, which is not easy for someone who, like the vast majority of humanity, is used to lying to themselves. Only someone, like me, who for 35 years has observed the enormous admiration of newcomers here who do not find what they had imagined, or rather, who find themselves here in the same condition as they were their whole lives, can evaluate this. Therefore, if something, or much, was done wrong, to know this there is no need for a “final judgment”. The judgment is already made, as a necessity, of chemical or physical characteristics, directs every place or department without allowing tergiversation.

I am, therefore, in Hell, but not far from a situation, or sphere, that can be considered the “lowest plane of Heaven”. It should not be forgotten that the terms “internal condition” and “external way of life” are not rigid, but flow into one another. I perceived the great light, which, in addition to the already mentioned justice, love and spiritual enlightenment, contains a series of other elements such as an enormous attractive force, which is not only an attraction to itself, but at the same time a true declaration of love. To respond to it, I not only began with the maximum intensity possible for me, to draw near to that world of love that attracts me. From this picture, naturally, the figure of Martha cannot be separated, who calls me as never before.

So, I try to do everything necessary for this inner transformation. Recognition and evaluation of the injustice committed have already happened. They are the premise and the first step to be taken by the penitent. I gradually came to this recognition, just as any modification in the Beyond is slow and gradual. One feels a vague displeasure until one has the will to analyze the past without prejudice. As this happened, I try to find a way to compensate for my failings in the form of service that I must render.
Altruism is the characteristic of the awakening soul. The “being there” for others, the end of that self-centered life. Self-detachment is both the path and the goal, as Lao-Tzu already stated.

I haven't started this “serving” yet. I still have to free myself from the pressure of what has not been expressed or made public. This has now happened. I thank you, Eva. I hereby revoke what I said about non-publication. I now leave you, to go to a cloister, or seminary, where I hope to make rapid progress. Farewell.”
Sigmund Freud
__SUB__Conclusion
Rightly, readers may question the credibility to be given to the text.
For greater clarity in basing your analysis, the study of the introduction to The Spirits' Book is essential. We reproduce a small pertinent excerpt on the matter.

__QUOTE__As for spirits who adorn themselves with respectable names, they betray themselves by their language and their maxims; he who would call himself Fénelon, for example, and who would offend, if only accidentally, good sense and morality, would thereby show the fraud. If, on the contrary, the thoughts are pure, without contradiction and constantly at the height of Fénelon's character, there is no reason to doubt his identity; otherwise it would be necessary to suppose that a spirit who preaches only good, can consciously preach falsehood, and that without utility.||Allan Kardec

visao-espiritual-fobias-panico-depressao: Unlike the traditional view, Reincarnation Psychotherapy brings a very different understanding from the traditional view regarding phobias, panic, severe depression, chronic physical pain, etc., which are seen as diseases, pathologies, disorders, in short, something bad. This new understanding sees these symptoms as an attempt by our Spirit to exonerate, “bring out,” traumatic situations from other incarnations, in its attempt to cleanse itself, to purify itself and, for this, it needs to free itself from everything it acquired while here on Earth, and these situations, which lie hidden within the Unconscious, then need to be eliminated. The symptoms are bad, but the intention of our Spirit is good, it is beneficial, provided that the therapist collaborates with it and assists the Spiritual Mentors in opening people's Unconscious so that this drainage can occur, and this is what Therapeutic Regression does through the ABPR Method. The traumatic situations, our past pains, what we need to eliminate from within our Unconscious, are chosen by the Spiritual Beings; we are their assistants, provided that we have true humility, a necessary detachment to obey them, and a courageous submission to occupy this place.

Medications to lower adrenaline and increase serotonin give a false sense of improvement, but traumatic situations remain within the Unconscious wanting to come out; therefore, people improve with chemical treatments but do not feel truly well. All of us carry traumas, fears, sadness, pain, in our Unconscious, from other centuries, and this is hidden within, they are “impurities” that need to be eliminated so that our Spirit can “cleanse” itself of them, in its search for frequency elevation. When the time comes for us to free ourselves from these situations, they begin to approach the dividing line that separates the Unconscious from the Conscious, wanting to emerge, seeking to come out from within, so that we can free ourselves from them. The person then begins to feel the symptoms that are wanting to emerge, such as fear, anguish, sadness, loneliness, physical pain, and this is diagnosed as Phobia, Panic Disorder, severe Depression and is also the origin of many chronic physical pains without a firm diagnosis and without a truly convincing treatment, such as Fibromyalgia. In other words, an attempt to cleanse our Unconscious is called a “disease,” and medications are used that only palliate but keep the situations inside, instead of opening a space for them to come out and everything to be resolved, usually quickly, without medication.

People who feel these symptoms are having the opportunity to finally free themselves from these traumatic situations, these fears, these anguishes, these pains, but for this to happen, serious, competent and responsible, humble, obedient and submissive professionals need to collaborate in this opening of their Unconscious and allow them to come out, to be exonerated. People suffering from Phobias, Panic, severe Depression, chronic pain, etc., can take the opportunity to free themselves from these situations from their past, not with charitable, generous, but merely palliative chemical medications, but rather with the exoneration of the psychopathological material that wants to come out. The Spirit wants to free itself from that, we assist, we are its allies and we help it to perform this cure, in a simple, fast and wonderful way, although still superficial, because the True Healing is the return of the Son to the Father, the return to Purification, because only the Pure remains Holy. We heal from the physical and psychological pains arising from the immersion in the illusions of life on Earth, until, one day, we truly heal, reintegrating into the Divine and disappearing into It.

Psychologists and psychiatrists, by not dealing with Reincarnation, a heritage of the non-reincarnationist Catholic conception post-Council of Constantinople, embark on a generally unfruitful search for the origin of these symptoms, in their patient's childhood or recent past, and the treatment consists of therapy sessions, specific techniques, and chemical medications, which frequently relieve symptoms, improve people's lives, but rarely promote a real cure. Everything remains inside, wanting to come out, but cannot do so alone; it needs someone to help open the basement door so it can emerge. True Faith, the surrender of one's will to God, the true reconnection through the heart, can promote this, but it is still inaccessible to most people, so Regression Sessions are necessary for their Spiritual Mentors to do what they could do alone.

Often, nightmares are spontaneous regressions, but at the critical moment of the situation, the person wakes up startled, believing it was a bad dream, but it wasn't, and it's what will often appear in Regression sessions. But then, the recollection will overcome the critical moment and, in our method, it will extend to the recollection of death in the past life that was accessed, the memory of disincarnation and the ascent to the Spiritual World, until we perceive that everything has passed, that all the resonances of that incarnation have already passed, the Optimum Point, and then the Regression ends, or another incarnation arises and the procedure is repeated.

So, the symptoms are bad but the intention is good, and we collaborate with that intention, we open a crack and this material can come out from within. It is a cure for residues from other incarnations, and we can use this current incarnation to promote this exoneration or leave it for another, when the same attempt by our Spirit to free itself from this material will repeat itself.
depressao: __SUB__The traditional View

Depression is characterized by a great and constant disinterest in life, a lack of will to live, in which the person often feels unable to deal with the basic things of their daily life. Depression can lead to an inability to perform things, whether physically or mentally, and can manifest itself in childhood or begin or worsen in adolescence, but its onset is more common in adulthood. A person with Depression feels unable to cope with life, thinks that it is not worth living or fighting, and this leads them to want to distance themselves from everything and everyone, and may even attempt or commit suicide. It is a situation for which we should all be prepared, as it can happen to anyone, even someone in our family. The symptoms of a person with Depression can go completely unnoticed and we only become aware of the situation when the person actually attempts suicide. We must be aware of the symptoms that are a sign of Depression so that we know if we or someone around us suffers from this disease.

Depression is very common in current society, with a tendency to increase due to the dehumanization of modern society. Everyone feels sad from time to time and such feelings are normal, but Depression, as a psychiatric illness, is quite different: it is a disease like any other that requires treatment. Many people think they are helping a depressed friend by encouraging or even demanding attempts to react, distract themselves, to have fun to overcome negative feelings. The friend who wants to help should listen to someone who feels depressed and advise or seek a professional when they realize that the depressed friend is not just sad, that it is something greater than that. In fact, no one knows what a depressed person feels, only they themselves and perhaps someone who has gone through it. Sometimes, not even their psychiatrist knows; they recognize the symptoms and know how to treat them, but that doesn't mean they truly know the feelings and suffering of their patient and, often, their origin.
__SUB__The Reincarnationist View

Unable to cope with life: Life here on Earth is not easy, and if we do not remember that we are a passing Spirit here, with the purpose of finding the inferiorities of our ego and seeking to improve them, to one day return for Purification (pure becomes sane), for many people, life here becomes heavy, increasingly difficult; these are financial problems, health problems, family problems, problems with children, politics, wars, injustices, misery, violence; in short, so many things happen here that, frequently, people who reincarnated with a tendency to give up, to weaken, to become sad, react this way and enter sadness, into Depression. It seems that everything is very difficult, very laborious, very heavy, there is no solution, things lose their meaning, life begins to be suffering. And remembering that we are very close to the Umbral, that is, under the influence of that place. To these people, I want to ask: Do you remember who you are? Do you remember what you came here to do? Here is not “The Life”; here is a place of passage, where our Spirit (ourselves) came to spend a few decades and then ascend again to our Spiritual Home, then descend again, ascend, descend, ascend… Do you remember that? When we are up there, our higher chakras are activated and we reveal the best of ourselves; when we are down here, the opposite happens, our lower chakras are activated and we show the worst of ourselves. And what is the worst of ourselves? Some have a tendency to argue, to fight, to duel, a tendency to want to command, to order, others to obey, to submit, to belittle themselves; some hurt others, injure, humiliate, others have a tendency to hurt themselves, to become sad, to feel sorry for themselves; some want to show off, others want to hide; some are vain, others do not see their value; in short, all of us, here on Earth, show ourselves and others what we need to improve. Depressed people came with the Mission to stop depressing themselves, to stop feeling sorry for themselves, to stop thinking they are weak, incapable; this is their proposal for Inner Reform and a Treatment with Reincarnationist Psychotherapy, showing them, through Inquiry into the Unconscious Sessions, that they have been like this for many lives, which can help them successfully fulfill this task.

It's not worth living: There's no point in wanting to die, because we never die! I remember an Inquiry into the Unconscious Session in which a person died of sadness in a past life, alone, locked in his room. They buried his body, and he went with it; he didn't leave the body. Inside the coffin, he said: “I'm going to die. I don't want to think anymore. I don't want to suffer anymore!” Time passed, he waited to die and didn't die, time passed and he didn't die. Spiritual Beings came to get him, to remove him from there, he said he would stay there, because he wanted to die, he wanted everything to end. And he didn't die, and nothing ended. Until, after a long time, seeing that he really wasn't going to die, that he wasn't going to stop thinking, he gave up wanting to die, accepted help, was taken to the Spiritual World, to a hospital, received treatment, improved, joined a study group for depressed people, learned what life on Earth was for, why he had been reincarnating for several lives, what his Inner Reform proposal was (not to depress oneself), until one day he reincarnated, returned to Earth. When he was in his early 40s, he came to consult with me, do you know for what? Depression. He had felt a sadness since childhood, a discouragement, a desire to isolate himself, to close himself off, to do nothing, to die... All over again. And here's some advice for suicides: if you think life here is bad, heavy, that it's not worth living, fighting, striving, you want to die, stop thinking, stop suffering, end everything, kill your physical body and see what happens... Either you'll wander here on Earth, or you'll stay inside the coffin, or you'll go down to a dark, smelly, sticky place, from which it's not easy to get out, or you'll ascend to the Spiritual World as weak, unsuccessful people, where you'll be ashamed of your weakness, your cowardice, your egocentric selfishness, and after a while you'll descend again with the repercussions of the damage you caused to your perispirit according to the way you killed yourselves, and you'll have to face everything again, and do you know what will emerge from you on the new descent? Sadness, discouragement, a desire to isolate yourself, to do nothing, to want to stop thinking, to suffer, to end everything. All over again.

Attempting or committing suicide: The ideas and attempts of suicide are caused or incremented by the non-reincarnationist conception that states that here is “The Life,” that we did not exist before and that afterwards we will spend the rest of the time doing nothing, waiting for the Last Judgment… In other words, this life is the only one; if you were born rich, it was luck; if you were born poor, it was bad luck; if you were born beautiful, it’s genetics; if you were born ugly, likewise; Brazilian is Brazilian, American is American, Iraqi is Iraqi, Jew is Jew, Arab is Arab; in short, everything is as it is, we are what we are, there are no Divine Laws regulating our birth, where we are, how we are, what race we belong to, in what family, skin color, in what family religion we will come; everything is chance, nothing is co-incidental, nothing is synchronistic. This view creates and aggravates racism, hegemonic struggles, wars for power, and also Depression, as it removes the meaning of life, the purpose of existence, and increases suicidal ideation and actions.

Western Psychology, Psychiatry, and Philosophy, following the non-reincarnationist conception originating from the Councils of the 6th-7th centuries, fail to transmit to their patients and to people in general a perspective, a deeper purpose for which to live, a real commitment to oneself, and, worse, they seek in childhood and in life the “villains” we must accept and forgive, they seek superficial stimuli for rekindling the joy of living, such as having fun, dancing, traveling, beautifying oneself, having plastic surgery… In reality, the true stimulus to rediscover the will to live and fulfill our Mission as an incarnated Spirit is to remember who we are, what we are doing here, what life on Earth is, what the purpose of passing through here so many times is, what our goal is, our objective, how to be victorious over ourselves! And the reincarnationist view can provide this, and psychologists, psychiatrists, and philosophers who embrace this view will become much better professionals and, in addition to all their methods and techniques, will be able to talk with their patients and people in general about Life.

Depression is very common in current society, with a tendency to increase due to the dehumanization of modern society: Why is our society becoming increasingly less human? Because life ends up becoming a “Every man for himself!”, with everyone running back and forth, on autopilot, living they don't even know for what, trying to be increasingly modern, responding to the appeals of television, fashion, technological evolution, progress, body, clothes, utilities, superficial internet communication, while at the same time we are closing ourselves off in our existential solitude, increasing our egocentric selfishness, living more and more for ourselves and our kin, working at anything, in any job, no matter if it's to produce and sell cigarettes, alcoholic beverages, no matter if it pollutes the environment, if it increasingly superficializes people, if we become increasingly childish, inconsequential, anxious, eat meat, kill animals, cut down forests, desertify the world, melt glaciers, nothing matters, the point is to live, enjoy life, be happy, after all, life is one… And so we feel less and less part of a Whole to be more and more selfish and less human, more dissatisfied, more depressed.

Advise: I will give my advice here, my recipe: let us take advantage of our incarnation, perceive our flaws, our inferiorities, our imperfections, what we must improve in ourselves, how we can evolve personally, existentially, spiritually, how we can help others, how we can help the world to improve, help our society to become increasingly just, more honest, more ethical, help end misery, social injustice, racism, wars, select the programs that deserve to be watched on television, what deserves to circulate on the internet, it is Ahimsa, the “Pacific Indignation” preached by Gandhi. We can eliminate our egocentric selfishness, our sadness, our boredom, by engaging in religious, humanitarian, social causes. Let us take advantage of the time to live on Earth in a spiritual way.

I remember once when I attended a lady who told me she suffered from Depression. She spent every day, most of the time, at home, locked in her room, lying down, watching television, reading, sleeping, with no desire for anything. The various psychological and medicinal treatments did not yield results, she had already consulted at Centers and there were no obsessive Spirits harassing her. In the Inquiry into the Unconscious Sessions we conducted, she detached from several depressive situations in other incarnations, but she did not improve, did not get out of bed, did not dispose herself to live… Since she had seen her Congenital Personality in several previous lives, and in this one, a strong tendency for Depression, I told her I would give her a medicine that would cure her! I told her that, instead of lying down, in her room, watching TV, reading, suffering for herself, and since she had detached from several past lives of Depression and undergone dis-obsessive treatment at a Spiritist Center, she should get in touch with some social work and offer to work from Monday to Saturday, mornings and afternoons, and within a month she should schedule a follow-up appointment with me to tell me how she was. She agreed and asked for the prescription. I told her I had already given her the prescription: the advice. She never came back. Either she accepted the recommendation and was cured, or she got mad at me.
__SUB__Symptoms of Depression
__SUB__The traditional view

The symptoms are very varied, ranging from feelings of sadness, through negative thoughts to changes in bodily sensation, such as pain and nausea. However, for diagnosis, a group of central symptoms is necessary:

__LIST__Loss of energy, fatigue, loss of interest, feeling sad and down, unable to find anything that cheers them up or arouses their interest||Depressed mood||Poor school performance, inability or great difficulty concentrating||Changes in appetite and sleep||Slowness in both physical and mental activities||Feeling of sorrow||Feeling of failure||Pessimism||Difficulty making decisions||Difficulty starting tasks||Irritability or impatience||Restlessness||Thinking life is not worth living; desire to die||Crying for no reason or difficulty crying||Feeling that things will never get better, hopelessness||Difficulty finishing tasks started||Feeling sorry for oneself||Persistence of negative thoughts||Frequent complaints and lamentations||Unjustifiable feelings of guilt||Dry mouth, constipation, weight and appetite loss, insomnia, loss of sexual desire||Withdrawal from friends or people||Desire to be alone||Withdraws from everything and everyone||Lack of desire to perform a certain task that progressively spreads or can spread to many other activities||Not wanting to hear noises or wanting music, loud noises, which is a way of detaching and distancing oneself from what is happening around them||Abusing medication, alcohol or drugs, means to distance oneself from what is happening around them||Fear of performing certain tasks or fear of what might happen if they fail, obsessed with their incapacity or with what might happen to others if they fail||Headaches, great tension or discomfort in the back, shoulders or head||Does not feel good anywhere||Lack of interest, carelessness with dressing or personal presentation.

To state that a patient is depressed, we must confirm that they feel sad most of the day, almost every day, no longer enjoy or have as much interest in activities they previously enjoyed, cannot sit still or, conversely, move more slowly than usual. They begin to have inappropriate feelings of hopelessness, despising themselves as a person and even blaming themselves for the illness or the problems of others, feeling like a dead weight in the family, and with this come thoughts of suicide, of ending everything. This picture must last at least two weeks for us to say that the patient is depressed.
__SUB__The reincarnationist view

Loss of energy, fatigue, loss of interest, feeling sad and down, unable to find anything that cheers them up or arouses their interest: These symptoms generally come from past life situations, where the person is stuck in a chain, where they remained until death, or was a very old person, alone, in their house, lying in a bed, or in a cell, in a gutter, or was a beggar, or died and went to the Umbral, where they remained like that for a long time, depressed, thrown in a corner, in the mud and is still attuned there. With antidepressant medication, their serotonin increases and the depressive person improves from these symptoms, but continues attuned to their past, where they are still like that. We never interrupt medicinal treatment, while performing Reincarnationist Psychotherapy and Inquiry into the Unconscious Sessions, people can take antidepressants and Inquiry into the Unconscious Sessions occur naturally, without any harm. Medications are palliative benefits, Inquiry into the Unconscious Sessions cut the evil at the root.

Feeling of sorrow: Frequently, the feeling of sorrow, which seems to have come from the current life, since losing one's father, mother, husband, child, etc., has its origin in other incarnations, in similar situations. And the current event was a reinforcement for what was already with them from the past. The person reincarnated with this “breach” in their psyche, that of feeling more than normally an affective loss, a sorrow. And in the current life, when a fact similar to what happened (and is still happening in their Unconscious) occurs, the feelings of today are added to those of then and the Depression is very strong, often resistant to antidepressants. With the detachment from past situations, the improvement is very great and immediate, and the psychiatrist who treats them can gradually reduce the antidepressants, and even, later, remove them. Electroshock or medications to deactivate the brain are true crimes against common sense; the psychiatrist is well-intentioned but would be even more so if, not curing the person of Depression, they followed Dr. Freud's guidance and investigated their Unconscious, where past lives are found and, in the vast majority of cases, the origin of Depression resistant to conventional treatments.

Feeling of failure: This feeling, that of failure, generally has its origin in one or more situations in past lives, where the person truly failed, made a mistake, did something wrong. And they come with this in their Unconscious, often with a fear of making mistakes, of failing, and if, by chance, this actually happens, the feeling of today combines with that of then and Depression sets in, the fear of trying again, often associated with guilt, a desire to do nothing more for fear of making mistakes.

Pessimism: I have conducted Inquiry into the Unconscious Sessions with people who were considered “pessimists” and they accessed situations in past lives where things really went wrong, failed, they lost material possessions, money, land, family members, and this was registered in their Unconscious, so that, today, they have a negative view of things, they think it will go wrong, that everything is difficult, that it can go wrong, and they are called pessimists. In fact, they are here, in the current life, and there in their past, where things really went wrong. The same happens with people labeled as “hypochondriacs,” who are attuned to past lives, to severe, incapacitating, fatal diseases. They are not hypochondriacs, they are here and there, and can be disconnected from there.

Difficulty making decisions: A young man came for a consultation and told me that he is a confident, hardworking person, a leader in his city, an entrepreneur, but that, deep down, he has a fear of taking risks, of doing something different, and since childhood he has felt a sadness that he doesn't know where it comes from. He tells me that it seems he has a fear of making a mistake, of hurting his family. In the Inquiry into the Unconscious Session, he accessed a past life in which he was a man, married, with children, who lived in the countryside and, one day, returning home by cart, it was raining heavily, and he decided to take another path, through the mountains, to shorten the journey, to arrive earlier, but he skidded, the cart fell, and he died! He left his body, went to his house, where he saw his wife and children waiting for him, when a man on horseback arrived to announce that he had suffered an accident, and he watched in panic, in terrible anguish, his wife and children go to where he was, dead! As a Spirit, he stayed in his house until his wife and children died, only then did he accept to ascend to the Spiritual World, where he recovered, got well, but reincarnated with that trauma from that life within his Unconscious. And in his current life, even as a child, he already felt that sadness, that fear, that anguish, and today, as an adult, married, he felt that fear of making mistakes, of doing something different, of taking risks, of hurting his family members. With this Inquiry into the Unconscious Session, he found out where this came from, understood what that fear of making mistakes, of taking risks, was, and detached himself from that situation. What other therapy can do that?

Feeling that things will never get better, hopelessness: Where can such a strong feeling come from in someone who is sick, that they will not get better, a hopelessness, is it the end? First, for having undergone several psychological and psychiatric treatments with little or no result, for having talked countless times about their childhood, their father, their mother, their husband or wife, their children, their life, what they feel. Second, for having the impression that they are being seen as a failure, spoiled, lazy, good-for-nothing. Third, because they have already undergone treatments in Spiritist Centers, and there are no obsessing spirits... But why then do they not improve? They are attuned to past lives of Depression and must detach themselves from there.

Feeling sorry for oneself: Within the notion of Congenital Personality, the basis of Reincarnationist Psychotherapy, this is one of the most common complaints and, at the same time, one of the most common Inner Reform proposals we find in people. It is found in people who have reincarnated with an old tendency to hurt themselves, feel sorry for themselves (a), victimized, rejected (a), unhappy. Generally, people who have reincarnated with this pattern say that since childhood they were already like this, not as much, but they already felt it within themselves. For a therapist who believes in and deals with Reincarnation, they are saying that they were like this in their previous life and, probably, in the one before that, and the one before that… Why did they reincarnate now? What is their Inner Reform proposal? How can they truly take advantage of this current incarnation in that sense? What if they don't take these “time travels,” the Inquiry into the Unconscious Sessions of our School, guided by their Spiritual Mentors, to remember that they have been like this for several past lives and to detach themselves from there? And what if they spend their whole life thinking that this was caused by their father and mother? They need to understand that they are like this because they were born like this. Is there anything more obvious than that? The other day, I saw a Superinteressante magazine at a newsstand where the cover story was: “Why are we like this?” and it talked about various theories to explain why we are the way we are, from the genetic hypothesis, to the family, social, neurochemical, etc. It just didn't mention the obvious hypothesis: we are like this because we were born like this, because that was our personality in the previous incarnation, and there we find our Inner Reform proposal.

Unjustifiable feelings of guilt: Sometimes, in Inquiry into the Unconscious Sessions, if the Spiritual Mentors deem it appropriate, the person who reports an “unjustifiable” feeling of guilt discovers that it was not unjustifiable: they killed people in a past life, stole, raped, abandoned their family, committed errors, mistakes. And even today they still feel that guilt from there. But the Inquiry into the Unconscious must be commanded by the Spiritual World and the recognition of people should never be encouraged, to prevent the therapist, even with good intentions, from allowing the person to access facts, recall situations they should not access, which were closed, forbidden, thus infringing the Law of Forgetfulness. Sometimes I am asked: if the person was not to recognize someone, in the case of therapists who commit this infraction, why do they access it, why does this happen, why do their Spiritual Mentors nearby allow it? They allow it because, by the Law of Free Will, everyone can do what they want, people and therapists. We do not command the Inquiry into the Unconscious Sessions precisely to avoid this: people seeing and knowing what was forbidden, what they could not and should not access.

Withdrawal from friends or people: Depressed people, especially chronic ones, tend to withdraw from other people, friends, and family, because everything loses its charm, they lose motivation, joy, disposition, enthusiasm, they begin to be ashamed of their condition, not wanting to laugh or play, and others often begin to feel sorry for them, to comment. In short, a whole picture that can last many, many years or even a lifetime. My advice: in addition to conventional medications, undergo Inquiry into the Unconscious Sessions and a consultation at a Spiritist Center.

Fear of what might happen if they fail, obsessed with their incapacity or with what might happen to others if they fail: As we mentioned previously, these fears, these ideas, fears, blockages that often last a lifetime, generally have their origin in failures committed in the past, when something bad happened to themselves or to other people.

Does not feel well anywhere: There are many people who feel like strangers in their own nest. Even within their family, they don't feel like they belong. They don't make friends, they are isolated, lonely, and even if they live with family, have friends, a social life, deep down they don't feel part of life, they fulfill their obligations because they have to, but they would like to be alone, at home, in silence. In Inquiry into the Unconscious Sessions, they generally see that they were solitary, isolated people in past lives, who only felt good after disincarnating, there in the Spiritual World, and who wanted to stay there. This tendency towards solitude is what they came to improve, within their Inner Reform proposal, but without knowing this and without detaching themselves from the loneliness of past lives, it is very unlikely that they will achieve this goal.

Ending everything: Ending everything is what suicides wish for, but this is impossible, because we are not our physical body, we are inside it, embedded in it, and death only makes us leave it, alive, thinking, feeling, or makes us remain in the dead body, as happens with some people who die, but do not leave, go to the coffin and stay there. We must have the notion that we are a Spirit in a physical body, which exists only so that we can experience passages through this dense planet, a body suitable for the earthly conditions of gravity, pressure and atmospheric conditions. Here on Earth there is oxygen, so our body must have a nose, bronchi and lungs to aspirate it and send it to the blood to make our cells work. We have to eat, so our body must have a mouth, esophagus, stomach, intestine and anus for what is left to come out. We have to eliminate liquids, so we must have kidneys, ureters, bladder and urethra. We must have a centralizing organ that receives external stimuli and sends them to our Spirit, which receives the stimuli and passes them on to the body, so we have the brain. We must have protection, so the body has skin, to which also goes what “is no good” (as people say: “put it out.”), that is why there are no skin diseases, but diseases in the skin. The cure for psoriasis, herpes, vitiligo, all chronic skin diseases, must be done internally, in people's thoughts and feelings, which is where they originate, and often from traumas from other lives. “Ending everything” is impossible, it will never happen, so there's no point in killing the physical body. What you have to do is discover where your Depression comes from, if there are no obsessive Spirits around, if you are not being too selfish, if you are not too materialistic, if your life has a spiritual meaning, if you practice charity, if you are not wasting too much time with nonsense, too much television, idle talk, if you are not addicted to drugs like alcohol, cigarettes or others, in short, if you are living as your Spirit wants, as your Spiritual Mentors want, or if you are simply surviving.
(extracted from the book “Treating Phobia, Panic and Depression with Past Life Regression Therapy” – Mauro Kwitko – Besourobox Edições)
fibromialgia: __SUB__Traditional view

Fibromyalgia is a mysterious syndrome of still unknown causes, characterized by severe body-wide pain or sensitivity in joints, muscles, and tendons over a long period. The most accepted hypothesis is that fibromyalgia is caused by stimuli being captured and interpreted abnormally by the brain; that is, a simple hug or a firm handshake can trigger these pains. This happens due to an alteration in the interpretation of stimuli received by the brain and also by cutaneous receptors. This occurs because repeated stimulation of the nerve that signals pain (neurotransmitter) modifies the brain.

It affects 2% to 10% of the world's population, predominantly young and middle-aged women (20 to 50 years old), with a ratio of seven women for every man. It is estimated that about 2% to 3% of Brazilians suffer from the condition in the country, and of these cases, 80% to 90% are women aged between 30 and 50.

The most visible signs of this syndrome are: generalized pain spread throughout the body and joints, which can last for months; fatigue and tiredness during the day; impaired sleep, in some cases the patient presents with apnea or insomnia; cognitive problems and altered memory, transforming a simple task requiring attention or concentration into something difficult to perform.

Diagnosis is clinical, made through anamnesis and specific physical examination. Laboratory and imaging tests are only requested to rule out other conditions that might confuse the diagnosis or be co-occurring with fibromyalgia.

Treatment consists of using medications such as antidepressants, anxiolytics, analgesics, and muscle relaxants, and regular practice of physical activities such as aerobics and anaerobics. Physiotherapy, Pilates, and muscle strengthening are great allies in alleviating the symptoms of the disease.

So far, research has not been able to determine the cause of Fibromyalgia, but it likely involves a variety of factors working together. There may be genetic causes, as cases of Fibromyalgia tend to run in families. There may be certain genetic mutations that make the carrier more susceptible to developing the disorder. However, the gene causing the disease has not yet been discovered.

Fibromyalgia is a chronic rheumatic syndrome characterized by pain throughout the patient's body, even without a physical injury to the muscles or joints. Regarding its history, the main symptom of the syndrome – diffuse musculoskeletal pain – was already described by Hippocrates, considered the father of Medicine, in the late 400s and early 300s BC, but it was only in 1824 that the association between rheumatism and painful points occurred, through studies by the Scottish physician and botanist John Hutton Balfour. Several descriptions of the syndrome can be found since the mid-19th century, but it was only recognized as such by the World Health Organization (WHO) in the late 1970s.

There is still no exact and specific cause for the onset of Fibromyalgia, but studies show a certain relationship between the start of symptoms soon after a traumatic event, which can be physical, psychological, or a severe infection. In most cases, the pain begins at a specific point in the body and becomes chronic, subsequently progressing to the rest of the body.

Precisely because of this lack of identifiable cause, Fibromyalgia is considered a syndrome, before being a disease, as it is a set of signs, symptoms, and medical problems that tend to occur simultaneously but are not related to a specific identifiable cause. However, even without knowing exactly the cause of the condition, what cannot be debated is whether the patient's pain is real or not. Research proves that people suffering from Fibromyalgia genuinely feel the pains they describe.
__SUB__Reincarnationist view
__SUB__A mysterious syndrome of still unknown causes

Fibromyalgia does not have an unknown cause; after 20 years of work, I assure you that it comes from traumatic (physical) situations from past lives to which the person is still attuned, feeling today the pains of that past. Generally, 4 or 5 situations (4 or 5 past lives) are responsible for the pains they feel today. When they were there, they knew what was happening: an accident, a fall, a stab, a spear, a shot, torture, etc. Today, they feel the pains and don't know where they come from... They come from your past, hidden within your Unconscious, which can be opened, in an ethical and responsible manner, with the person's Spiritual Mentors allowing access to these situations, one by one, to recall them until their end, until the end of that life, to recall the Inter-life Period, in which everything that came with them from Earth was processed, passing, until it disappeared (Optimal Point); that past life no longer bothers them, and we move on to another, until all the sources of pain have been disconnected, they no longer feel any pain, everything has passed, then the Mentors begin to show good, useful, productive lives, signaling that a new way of living your life today can begin.
__SUB__An alteration in the interpretation of stimuli received by the brain and also by cutaneous receptors

As traditional Medicine, self-proclaimed “official,” studies and works only with our physical body, being the best Medicine for urgencies and emergencies, to save our lives, but it stops there, it has not yet entered our other, energetic bodies (this will happen in the coming decades), it functions like a mechanical workshop, where the vehicle is treated but not the driver. With this, diseases are seen as:
__LIST__Caused by external agents (microorganisms, chemical agents, etc.)||Of cerebral origin||Of genetic origin||Without identifiable cause

Fibromyalgia is not an alteration of the brain's interpretation of what is happening; it's not even a disease. It's a tuning to the past in people whose Unconscious wants to “let it out,” so those old traumas approach the Unconscious-Conscious boundary (to make a poor comparison, like a boil), wanting to emerge, and the person begins to feel those pains. That's when their pilgrimage of consultations, diagnoses, exams, and palliative treatments begins; meanwhile, the “boil” is right there on the verge of breaking through. The solution? Open the Unconscious and let it out. But will big pharmaceutical companies like to stop earning millions and millions of dollars? So they fund medical conferences where everything is discussed except the true cure, which is easily achieved without medication.
__SUB__Seven women for every man

Actually, in these 20 years, I don't recall any man coming for Fibromyalgia consultation, only women. I don't know why, but it's a fact. Do men not come? Or is it really a disorder that is more related to the female sex? Are women more sensitive, do they allow themselves to feel pain, seek help? I really don't know, but one day we will know.
__SUB__Laboratory and imaging tests only to rule out other conditions
This is important for making a good diagnosis, ruling out other hypotheses (rheumatic, endocrinological, psychological, etc.).
__SUB__Treatment

Now we enter a controversial area. All Treatment should aim for a cure, not just palliate, alleviate symptoms. If a type of Treatment cannot cure, the doctor should look for other types of Treatment, other Medicines, even if they are not considered official, recognized by the Medical Council, but, of course, that have foundation, proven efficacy. Every day a new wonderful, magical Medicine emerges, which its creator (often “channeled”) proclaims fantastic cures, true miracles. In addition to the religious “cures” sold at high prices by Churches that commercialize Jesus.
__SUB__Allies to alleviate the symptoms of the disease

It is important to have physical exercises directed by professionals specialized in Fibromyalgia, to work the musculature, joints, and tendons of these suffering people. They are excellent allies. There are also energetic procedures, new Medicines, which, in the hands of competent professionals who know what they are doing, act beneficially in these cases; some even claim cures, I don't know them, I only know how to seek the origin of the pains in the past and disconnect the person from there.
__SUB__Cases of Fibromyalgia tend to occur in families

What is a family if not a grouping of Spirits united by ties of affinity or divergence? So, in the same family there can be people who went through the same circumstances in the distant past, but there can also be the victim from there and the villain... One feels the pain that affected them, the other feels the pain they caused, and they are here again to see if this time they will resolve things. Although, often, someone was a victim in one life and a villain in another, and vice-versa.
__SUB__Chronic rheumatic syndrome

Since official Medicine deals only with the consequences that appear in people's physical bodies, often the “chronic character” is due to this: not acting on the cause, on the origin, but only on the physical resonances of internal, deep issues. Fibromyalgia is not rheumatism, nor does it need to be chronic; just follow Dr. Freud's guidance, updated for the 21st century.
__SUB__Hippocrates

Hippocrates (460 BC – 377 BC) was a Greek physician, born on the island of Kos, off the coast of Asia Minor. He was the son of Heraclides and Fenareta, descendants of Asclepius, the Greek god of Medicine, on his father's side, and of Hercules on his mother's side. He was said to be a member of a secret society called the Asclepiads, the children of Asclepius, which gathered wise men and scholars.

Hippocrates traveled extensively, teaching Medicine in the places he visited. The School of Hippocrates, on the island of Kos, probably succeeded the one established in the 6th century BC by the Greek mathematician Thales. The School taught not only the principles of Medicine but also appropriate personal relationships between doctor and patient. Before Hippocrates, the practice of Medicine was in the hands of the priests of Aesculapius, the Greek and Roman god of healing. Illness was seen as the result of the gods' anger with men. The sick went to the temple of Aesculapius seeking the assistance of priests. Hippocrates denied the healing powers of the gods.

Hippocrates believed that diseases resulted from an imbalance between what he called the “Doctrine of the Four Humors”: “blood,” “phlegm” (state of mind), yellow bile, and black bile. For him, every body carries within it the elements for its recovery. But knowledge of the body is only possible through knowledge of man as a whole.

The following writings are attributed to Hippocrates: “Treatise On the Sacred Malady,” “On Airs, Waters, and Places,” “On Prognostics,” “Epidemics,” “Ancient Medicine,” “Aphorisms,” “On Surgery,” “On Fractures,” “On Joints,” “On Ulcers,” and the “Oath.”

Of fundamental importance is his ethics summarized in the famous “Hippocratic Oath: “I swear to fulfill, to the best of my ability and judgment, this covenant: I will respect the hard-won scientific gains of those physicians in whose steps I walk, and gladly share such knowledge as is mine with those who are to follow. I will apply, for the benefit of the sick, all measures which are required, avoiding those twin traps of overtreatment and therapeutic nihilism. I will remember that there is art to medicine as well as science, and that warmth, sympathy, and understanding may outweigh the surgeon's knife or the chemist's drug. I will not be ashamed to say “I know not,” nor will I fail to call in my colleagues when the skills of another are needed for a patient's recovery. I will respect the privacy of my patients, for their problems are not disclosed to me that the world may know. Most especially must I tread with care in matters of life and death. If it is given me to save a life, all thanks. But it may also be within my power to take a life; this awesome responsibility must be faced with great humbleness and awareness of my own frailty. Above all, I must not play at God. I will remember that I remain a member of society, with special obligations to all my fellow human beings, those sound in mind and body as well as the infirm. If I do not violate this oath, may I enjoy life and art, respected while I live and remembered with affection thereafter. May I always act so as to preserve the finest traditions of my calling and may I long experience the joy of healing those who seek my help.”
These ideas are contained in the oath taken, to this day, by medical graduates.
The date of Hippocrates' death is uncertain; some biographers believe it was in 377 BC. He was buried in Larissa, Thessaly, Greece. His tomb was revered by people for many centuries.
__SUB__Onset of symptoms soon after a traumatic event

It is almost a general rule that after a traumatic event, a past life “awakens” and the person begins to feel its effects, physical or psychological, from then on. This occurs in relation to a phobia, panic, severe depression, and also in chronic physical pains, such as Fibromyalgia. It may happen that a person is already born with one or more past lives acting on their physical or psychic being (examples: young children with extreme shyness, strong feelings of rejection, low self-esteem, etc., or intense fears). Often what is called “night terror” are spontaneous regressions to traumatic situations from past lives, the same for some “nightmares”; but in these cases, it is good to investigate if there are no obsessive Spirits nearby negatively influencing during sleep.
__SUB__Are not related to a specific identifiable cause

Yes, they are. They are related to a specific identifiable cause through the recollection of past lives; there we find the cause and the solution (recall the situation until everything has ended, passed, when already in the Inter-life Period at what we call the “Optimal Point”). The person learns what the cause was and never again feels the resonance, whether on a physical or psychic level. But beware of unethical, egoic, vain Regression therapists, who are actually more merchants than therapists, only wanting fame and fortune, but they know how to convince desperate sufferers.
__SUB__People who suffer from Fibromyalgia really feel the pains they describe

Yes, it's true, they do feel it. It is not imaginary, it is not a hoax, it is not manipulation, it is not to get attention, it is not something for those who have nothing to do, it is not because they just stay at home, do not study, do not work, etc. The person suffering from these pains from the past still has to deal with ignorance and prejudice in relation to it.
transtorno-do-panico: __SUB__The traditional view

In the traditionally accepted view, panic disorder is a disorder distinctly different from other types of anxiety, characterized by sudden attacks, without apparent triggering factors and, frequently, with a tendency to incapacitate the person from leading a normal life. After a first panic attack, for example, while the person is driving, shopping in a crowded store or in some open place, a square, a soccer stadium, they can develop an irrational fear of these situations and start to avoid them. Gradually, the level of anxiety and the fear of a new crisis can reach such proportions that the person with panic disorder may even become unable to drive or leave the house. At this stage, the person is said to have Panic Disorder with Agoraphobia. Thus, panic disorder can have as great an impact on a person's daily life as other more serious illnesses, unless they receive effective treatment and are understood by others.

Panic disorder is defined as recurrent crises of severe anxiety or fear. Panic attacks are intense, sudden and unexpected, and cause in people a strong sensation of physical and mental discomfort, together with a behavior of fleeing the place where they are, whether by going to an emergency room, seeking help from those nearby, or wanting to return home.
