The panic reaction is a normal reaction when there is a situation that favors its emergence, for example, being in a confined space where a fire starts, drowning, or in any situation with imminent risk of death; in these cases, the sensation of panic is normal. But panic begins to be identified as pathological, and therefore is called panic disorder, when this same reaction occurs without apparent reason, spontaneously, and begins to repeat itself.

Patients with panic disorder seek professional help, without anyone being able to tell them the true origin of their ailments. The person goes through an endless series of consultations and exams, but their problem is rarely solved. About ¾ of people with this mental disorder are treated by cardiologists, neurologists, or other specialists, and not by a psychiatrist or psychologist. Most people with panic disorder do not treat their disease adequately, and a wrong diagnosis can lead these people to live with enormous discomforts that end up extending to their entire family.

The physical symptoms of a panic attack appear suddenly, without any apparent cause. The symptoms are as if the body is preparing for something terrifying. The natural reaction to danger is to activate our fight-or-flight mechanisms, where the organism tries to increase blood supply to the brain and limbs, to run away, to the detriment of other parts of the body. And the person may experience strong muscle tension, palpitations, dizziness, lightheadedness, nausea, difficulty breathing, dry mouth, chills or hot flashes, sweating, a feeling of dreaming or distortions of perception of reality, feeling terror, a sense that something unimaginably horrible is about to happen and that one is powerless to prevent such an event, confusion, rapid thinking, fear of losing control, fear of doing something embarrassing, fear of dying, vertigo, feeling of weakness, etc.

A panic attack lasts minutes or hours, and is a very distressing situation. Most people who have one attack will have others if they don't seek treatment. Psychological and medicinal treatment alleviates the attacks and can, eventually, make them disappear
__SUB__The re-incarnationist view
__SUB__1. Irrational fear

The panic that people feel is not irrational; on the contrary, it is genuine, truthful, but people, their family members, and the professionals who treat these disorders cannot find an explanation for the symptoms; they do not understand them. They search in childhood, they search in humanity's archetypes, they search in neurotransmitters, and they will never find it there, because panic disorder is an attunement to highly traumatic events from other incarnations. The fear called “irrational” occurs because, at that moment, the Unconscious identifies a situation similar to what the person has experienced in another era and which is still active. When the Unconscious senses that something similar to what happened in another incarnation could happen again, it activates all physiological mechanisms to flee or fight, and that's where the symptoms come from.
__SUB__2. Effective treatment

We fully agree with the need for “effective treatment,” but what is effective treatment? Traditionally, an effective treatment consists of two aspects: psychological and medicinal. Traditional psychological treatment, going only as far as childhood, and medicinal treatment, going only as far as neurotransmitters, rarely manage to be effective in terms of cure, not alleviation or suppression of symptoms. All Regression therapists in the world daily receive people who have been subjected to these “effective” treatments for many years, sometimes decades, and what is frequently achieved with these treatments is a partial, temporary improvement at best, until it becomes necessary to increase the psychotropic dose or switch to another, or try a more modern one, some latest generation…

Effective treatment, in our opinion and in that of thousands of doctors, psychologists, and alternative therapists worldwide, is to seek the origin of what caused the panic disorder, to go where it is. And in 100% of cases, in our statistics, it is in past lives, and if the person does not detach from there, one cannot speak of a truly effective treatment. What officially exists is an old-style allopathic treatment with palliative chemical remedies to lower adrenaline and increase serotonin, psychological treatments of various lines, etc., when this “disease” is a fixation in memory and can only be cured by going into memory.
__SUB__3. Be understood by others

People who do not believe in or deal with Reincarnation complain that they have no idea why they feel this way, why they have crises, where this fear, this terror, suddenly comes from; they want to understand their suffering and find a definitive solution. People who believe in Reincarnation complain that they know what they feel is not from this life, it is from past lives, but the psychologists and psychiatrists they have consulted do not believe or, if they believe, do not want to talk about it, and they want to be treated by professionals who deal with this past. Many reincarnationist psychologists and psychiatrists do not work with Regression Therapy because they understand that the Law of Forgetfulness must be respected. We, from ABPR (Brazilian Association of Reincarnationist Psychotherapy), also think this way, so we do not direct the recollection (regression), we only help the person to promote a relaxation of their physical body and an elevation of their frequency, so that their Spiritual Mentors direct the Inquiry into the Unconscious to the original situation of the panic, where she is still tuned in and which is activated when her Unconscious thinks that it will happen again... And we are totally against encouraging the recognition of people from the past, as is done by some regression therapists in Brazil and around the world, for being a very serious infraction of the Law of Forgetfulness.
__SUB__4. An escape behavior from the place one is in

At the moment the person is in a panic crisis and, at all costs, wants to flee the place where they are, escape, go home, seek help, go to an Emergency Room, they are no longer just here, in the present moment, they are also there in that past life, where all they wanted was really to flee the place where they were being attacked, where a tragedy was happening, where they were going to be lynched, hanged, they were in a place on fire, etc.

The “irrational”, “unprovoked” panic crisis is intense, dramatic, terrifying, because it is regressed, it is here and there in that incarnation, where the fear is really happening, only when it was there, it knew what it was, when it is here, in current life, that emerges from within your Unconscious, and you have no idea what it is! And worse, your psychologist and your psychiatrist don't either, except for psychologists and psychiatrists who believe in Reincarnation, but most of them don't dare to publicly assume their conviction and don't work with it in their practice.
__SUB__5. For no apparent reason
The reason is not apparent because it is hidden within the Unconscious. When a person panics, they have regressed to the past, and the reason is there.
__SUB__6. Without anyone being able to tell them the true origin of their afflictions

All Regression therapists who deal with Reincarnation are able to tell the true origin of their patients' ailments: traumas from past lives. And they are also able to greatly improve or cure them within a few months, if the patients' inner and outer forces allow it. The inner forces that can harm the treatment are usually feelings of guilt arising from other incarnations, which make the person believe that they cannot improve, heal, that they don't deserve it… The outer forces are the obsessing Spirits who are there, by their side, mentally influencing them to abandon the treatment.
__SUB__7. Mental disorder

What is a mental disorder? It is an alteration of the mind, consisting of strange, inexplicable thoughts, difficult or impossible to understand. But where is the mind? In the brain? No, the mind is in our 3rd body, the mental body, because we are an Energetic Being composed of several bodies. The physical body is temporary, disposable, the only easily visible one; the emotional body is the seat of feelings and sensations; and the mental body is the seat of thoughts. These and other even more subtle bodies constitute what religions call the Spirit. To beneficently affect a mental disorder, one must go to the mental body, the seat of thought, and chemical medications don't even come close to this, as they stop at the brain, and thoughts are not there. To reach thoughts, a procedure that aims to be curative must have the same subtlety as them, and that is energetic, or else disconnect harmful thoughts from their origin, which is what Regression does, acting directly on memory.
__SUB__8. A psychiatrist or psychologist

Each person should seek the mental and emotional health professional who fits their convictions, their way of seeing life, and we can divide these professionals into two categories: those who do not believe and those who do believe in Reincarnation. People who do not believe in Reincarnation should seek professionals who also do not believe in it and undergo treatments pertinent to that view. People who do believe should seek professionals who also believe, in order to investigate the origin of symptoms, conduct an Inquiry into the Unconscious and, if necessary, an investigation into their spiritual surroundings, in free Spiritist Centers. But a large number of people who have phobias or suffer from panic and believe in Reincarnation spend years, decades, treating themselves with professionals who do not believe, and, instead of trying to solve the problem at its root, they keep treating the symptoms.
__SUB__9. They do not treat your illness properly

The appropriate form is the form of each one, of each professional. For those who follow the traditional line, the appropriate form is to treat childhood, current life, and neurotransmitters. For alternative therapists, the appropriate form is to help open people's unconscious so that, from within, like a boil, what is bothering them comes out, rises to the surface, like a cleansing, an exoneration.
__SUB__10. As if the body were preparing for something terrible

It's not as if it were, it truly is a preparation of the body for something terrible! At the moment of panic, the person is truly in panic, there in that life where something terrible is going to happen or is happening! Those who have undergone or witnessed past life Regression know that we are living several lives concurrently, and in Regression, the past life that is bothering us comes to the surface, so it can be eliminated.
__SUB__11. Something unimaginably horrible is about to happen, and you are powerless to prevent it.

An attack by a powerful enemy army, by a rival tribe, the moment of one's hanging, of a rape, a terribly threatening fire - isn't that unimaginably horrible, where one is powerless to prevent such an event? How is it possible that a Council held in 553 AD managed to remove Reincarnation from some Religions and from Psychology and Psychiatry until today, and with that a huge possibility of effective cure for phobias, panic disorder, severe depressions, chronic physical pains, etc.?
__SUB__12. Fear of dying

A person regressed to a terribly threatening situation in a past life feels the enormous fear of dying they felt there. The panicked person is there in the past, with an enormous fear of dying, and even worse, at that moment their Unconscious knows that they actually died, or managed to survive, but it deeply affected them, to the point of bringing that trauma to today. The possibility of healing? To recall the situation, the death there, the disincarnation, their ascent to the Spiritual World, to remember that everything passed, improved, until remembering that they were well, and then we can conclude the recall: it is disconnected from a life originating from panic. But, generally, from our 22 years of experience with about 20,000 regressed people, there are 3 or 4 past incarnations that must be accessed, recalled, and disconnected from there. This can be done in 2 or 3 Inquiry into the Unconscious sessions. Without the need for years and years of treatment and without medication.
__SUB__Symptoms of Panic Disorder
__SUB__The traditional view

__LIST__Accelerated heart rate (or uncomfortable heart palpitation sensation), Sweating (diffuse or localized, mainly in hands or feet), and Tremors in hands, extremities, or diffuse throughout the body.||Sensation of suffocation or difficulty breathing||Sensation of imminent fainting||Chest pain or discomfort (leading many people to believe they are having a heart attack)||Nausea or abdominal discomfort||Dizziness, instability, sensation of lightheadedness or emptiness||Depersonalization||Derealization||Fear of going crazy or losing self-control||Fear of dying
__SUB__The reincarnationist view
__SUB__1. Accelerated heart rate, sweating, tremors

These classic symptoms are caused by an increase in adrenaline, which happens when we are in a stressful moment, in a dangerous situation, facing imminent death, or a very severe threat where we have to flee or fight. But that's not happening... Why is she like this, with tachycardia, sweating, trembling? It's because that's exactly what's happening in another life: she's there, in a panic attack. Here, looking around, nothing is happening, but there it is, and this is considered a symptom, but, in reality, it's what's truly happening there. Her pupils dilate, her heart races to supply more blood to the muscles, her breathing becomes labored, all due to the greater release of Adrenaline. It is our conditioning to survive if there is any real danger; it is an alarm system that is triggered without any obvious external danger. All of this is happening within the Unconscious.
__SUB__2. Feeling of suffocation or difficulty breathing

This symptom may stem from a situation of shortness of breath, a hanging, a death in a gas chamber in a concentration camp, an arrow shot or a sword blow to the neck in a past life, and suddenly, without an apparent reason, the person accesses and begins to feel what they felt there. They are here and they are there, usually more there than here.
__SUB__3. Feeling of imminent fainting
It is the moment in the past when the person feels they are going to fall or are falling and no longer have strength, they are fainting... Usually, after this moment, death comes in that past life.
__SUB__4. Chest pain or discomfort

Besides the action of substances released during a panic crisis that cause these symptoms, we have often heard in inquiry into the unconscious sessions, people reporting deaths by arrow, spear, gunshot, etc., to the chest and, at that moment, stating that it is exactly like the pain they feel when having a panic attack.
__SUB__5. Nausea or abdominal discomfort

Intense fear causes these symptoms. Sometimes it is exacerbated by something that happened in the original situation, such as seeing several injured, dead people, a lot of blood, physical or sexual abuse, etc.
__SUB__6. Dizziness, instability, feeling lightheaded or empty-headed

The moment fear dominates a person, we tend to want to flee the situation, and since we are not our physical body, but rather are in it, we might try to escape the body, and this attempt to leave can cause dizziness, instability, a strange sensation in the head, etc. Therefore, Dr. Bach, an English physician from the early 20th century, creator of Flower Therapy, created Rescue, a compound of 5 floral essences for moments of intense fear or panic, and among them is Clematis, which aims to keep us in the body, so we can act as we should, or can, act.
__SUB__7. Depersonalization

It is a common sensation in anxious states that can arise even outside of panic attacks. It is characterized by giving the person a feeling of not being themselves, as if they were observing themselves. This is not an unreal sensation, because, as we know, at that moment the person is 80-90% there in that life and 10-20% in this current one. There they were another person, hence the depersonalization. Let's say that here, their name is João, and there, their name was Peter. At the moment of panic, they are more Peter than João, this is psychiatrically called depersonalization, but it is not, it is them here and them there, much more there than here.
__SUB__8. Derealization

It is the feeling that the world or the surrounding environment is different, as if it were a dream or there was a cloud, everything becomes strange... Since, at the moment of a panic attack, the person is more in a past incarnation than in this one, the feeling that the world or the surrounding environment is different is real, they are indeed in another place. Here they are on the street, in the mall, at the movies; there in the other life, they are on a battlefield, in a prison, on a scaffold...
__SUB__9. Fear of going crazy or losing control of oneself

There, at the origin of the panic, she is going crazy with fear because she cannot prevent what is going to happen, has no power to change reality, cannot leave that place, has no possibility of altering that situation. She is very afraid, there is nothing she can do, it is imminent, she will die, she will be hanged, she will be raped, she has no control over what is happening or what will happen... But she is “here,” feeling the same thing.
__SUB__10. Fear of dying

The fear of dying is one of the strongest symptoms in a panic attack, and it is so strong, so dramatic, because the person is genuinely afraid, they might die at any moment or they know they are going to die, they are heading towards their death! But it's not a symptom, it's true, they really will die! There in that life... If they don't disengage from there with an Inquiry into the Unconscious session, how will they stop having these crises now and then? It's not a random release of neurotransmitters due to a brain imbalance, it's all true!
(extracted from the book "Treating Phobia, Panic and Depression with Past Life Regression Therapy" - Mauro Kwitko - Besourobox Edições)
jovens-guerreiros-da-luz-e-a-cannabis: Cannabis sativa is a herbaceous plant of the Cannabinaceae family, and its main psychoactive chemical compound is delta-9-tetrahydrocannabinol, commonly known as THC. It also contains other cannabinoids, such as Cannabidiol and Cannabinol, all of which are responsible for its effects on the Central Nervous System. Among these effects, which attract many people, are muscle relaxation, a feeling of calm, a certain drowsiness, an improvement in mood, an increase in optimism, a stimulation of creativity, and a greater or lesser euphoria.

The initial effects of using this plant cause a pleasant sensation, which captivates many people, as it calms, relaxes, internalizes, “problems disappear,” “annoyances lose their seriousness,” “nothing is as serious or urgent,” “everything has a solution,” and the use of this plant gradually becomes the solution for daily stress, for frustrations, for sadness, for irritation, for the boring things in life that must be done, to calm down during the day, to retreat inward at the end of the day, for when there's nothing to do, and its use gradually begins to intensify, because its effect is so good that there's no reason not to use it; after all, one becomes calmer, more peaceful, happier, so why not use it?

Many users of this plant claim that its use, due to the expansion of consciousness it provokes, brings with it a spiritual opening, a new vision of reality, a liberation from the materialistic information of our egoic and capitalist society, as if it were a reunion with oneself, with our spiritual identity, and for this reason it is considered a “sacred plant” and its use is defended as if it were a spiritual right, even based on freedom of worship and religious choice.

It is stated that this plant is “peaceful” in its message, that no one under its effect becomes violent or aggressive, that the person becomes more spiritual, calmer, more tranquil, more loving. And the comparison with alcoholic beverages, which are legalized and encouraged, and their disinhibiting effect, liberating hidden characteristics, which often degenerate into aggression, into self-destructive and heteroregressive attitudes, is one of the arguments of Cannabis users, and one cannot entirely deny them the reason behind this reasoning. Some users even argue that if everyone used Cannabis, violence on Earth would end. Maybe...

So, I'm thinking, perhaps the sacramental, occasional use of Cannabis could really help us eradicate our innate violence, domesticate the human being, tame our Ego, spiritualize us? A use, say, monthly or bi-monthly, in a special ritual, in a Full Moon Ritual, on a day of a special astrological configuration, with all respect, with reverence, perhaps, to access higher spiritual levels? But the daily use of this “sacred plant,” an everyday use, at any time, in any way, without any respect that the “sacredness” of this plant would then demand? Is this a sacramental use of a “sacred plant”? If this plant is indeed sacred, it should be treated as such by its users, but that is not what is seen, rather, it is a vulgar use, without respect for it. And, frequently, the spiritual opening referred to by many users in the early days of using this plant ends up degenerating into a merely theoretical experience, into egocentric spirituality, into a search for internal journeys of fantastic discoveries, into an egoistic exercise of seeking pleasure, of expanding the ability to perceive sounds and colors, aiming only to travel, travel and travel. The “spiritualization” can then transform into a mere theory, practice transforming into only spiritual ideas, and everything turning into a desire to internalize more and more, to be calm, peaceful, and only that, and this reminds me of a Buddhist story:

__QUOTE__Once a novice offered himself as a disciple to a monk in a temple in the mountains. The monk asked what he could do. The novice sat down and entered a meditative state. Time passed and the aspiring novice remained there, sitting, meditating, internalized… Days passed and he was still there, smiling, happy, meditating… At one point, the monk decided to interrupt that selfish exercise and asked if he wanted to help in the temple, sweep the floor, clean the kitchen, the bathroom… The candidate replied that he wanted to become enlightened, he preferred to stay there, meditating… The monk picked up the broom he had offered him for work and chased him out with broomsticks, saying that he already had enough stone Buddhas to adorn the temple!

The search for transcendence, the peace at the beginning of its use, the joy of liberation that the use of this plant brings initially, can, little by little, be transformed into self-neglect, into an insensitive or exaggerated affection towards other people, and practical spirituality, the one that truly matters, the one that yields an evolutionary result, based on the work of helping the needy, the poor, the sick, which demands discipline from our ego, which demands psychological and spiritual maturity, gives way to mere enjoyment, as if life were a succession of moments of relaxation and happiness, especially if the user relies on other people who work for them, who strive for their livelihood, who face the annoyances of daily life, who fulfill their social obligations, who do what they don't like to do, such as having commitments, punctuality at work, facing traffic, paying bills, etc., that is, one or more people bear the burden of hard work so that the user of this plant can be happy and enjoy their “spiritual high.”

And personal freedom, the liberation that this plant brings in its message, transforms into financial dependence, generated by the deactivation of the basic chakras (responsible for our grounding, our direction, our personal power), and by the external opening of the upper chakras, the spiritual chakras, not in a conscious way, with work, discipline, constancy, respect and education in relation to spiritual things, but in the way that perhaps many Cannabis users use it: smoking a joint, enjoying a high, in a group activity, among conversations not always of a high tone, of a spiritual nature, or in the morning to wake up, at any time of day to ward off boredom, during class breaks, camouflaged in a small square or on a street corner, at night to perform rarely elevated acts, in short, in an attitude totally opposite to the advertised character, to the sacred character of this plant.

The use of a “power plant” would require a minimum of respect for it, which is not observed in many of its users, who use it unregulated, routinely, daily, not to evolve spiritually, to evolve as a human being, but to be happy, to be light, to be well…

Many users of this plant gradually experience difficulty concentrating and achieving things, becoming experts in procrastination. Why? Because the daily use of this plant increases the number of THC receptors in the cerebral cortex, hippocampus, cerebellum, and basal ganglia of the brain, which leads to the harms associated with its abuse: alterations in thinking ability, difficulty in reasoning, memory deficiencies, and impairments in coordination and learning. As a consequence, what could, perhaps, be an exercise in spiritualization, in expanding personal power, in raising spiritual awareness, transforms into a dizzying fall in academic performance, work performance, creative potentials, and the performance of life itself, and the person gradually becomes a caricature of themselves, without realizing it.

Everyone sees the gradual decrease in their persistence, direction, hears their theoretical daydreams, and laments that they are wasting their potential. Everyone advises them, worries about them, but, dominated by the “peace” that comes from this plant, dominated by the influences of friends, and dominated by obsessive spirits, they don't see this, and continue smoking, and continue traveling, producing less and less or much less than they could, evolving less and less or much less than they should, and never truly become adults, perpetuating adolescence, but age catches up, time passes, opportunities slip away, until, one day, at 30 or 40, they wake up and see the time they lost, they kept seeking peace and joy, only peace and joy, without realizing that this is egocentrism and that spirituality is the work of giving to others, it is not theory, it is practice, it is distributed love, it is an outstretched hand, it is the attitude of living for the common good, it is liberating oneself, it is healing one's feelings and not masking them.

People who work all day and smoke at night at home to relax find this very good; after all, after a full day of work, why can't they smoke one and relax? But why do they need to smoke one to relax? It's the same as needing to take Valium, Dienpax, Frontal; they also calm you down. And when they smoke a joint and are at home tripping, are they doing something good for the world at those times? Are they helping an NGO, some association of people who want to improve an unjust situation? No, they are enjoying themselves… listening to wonderful music… having fantastic ideas… This is what can be called “the selfishness of wasted time.”

In fact, our capitalist system is not very keen on us using our time to improve situations created and provoked by its ideology; it prefers people to be distracted, to have fun, and for this, watching television, listening to music, chatting online, and other leisures are highly encouraged. The same encouragement isn't seen for helping others, for leaving home to collaborate in social works, in charitable institutions. And so the subtle message of “enjoying your time” is, in reality, an encouragement to selfishness, to egocentrism, to “passing time,” to “enjoying youth,” to “enjoying life.”

Marijuana has an effect that, with its misuse, daily, without reverence for a “power plant,” can become harmful to its users: losing focus. By expanding consciousness, it takes you to the astral plane, decreases grounding, which can make people have ups and downs in their will, an increasing instability in their direction, wanting something greatly, aiming for something, smoking a joint, the will passing… putting it off… They have a dream, they would very much like to achieve a goal, an objective, they smoke a joint, that's not so urgent anymore, I'll do it tomorrow… They want to reach a higher level in a talent, they see people with the same talent producing, excelling, they also want to be that, they smoke a joint, they imagine they already are… but they are not… they could be, but marijuana, by impairing the ability to achieve things, didn't let them… And frustration sets in… it could be this, it could be that, but they lost the 3 most necessary tools to achieve success: focus, persistence, and discipline. And marijuana can provoke the opposite of this; it takes you to the Astral, it artificially opens the upper chakras, while discipline, persistence, and focus are attributes of the earthly chakras.

And the use of Cannabis can then, from occasional, become routine, a daily act, a daily habit, and seem like the panacea for all ills has been found. But what the user doesn't see is that the pleasant relaxation turns into laziness, the calm transforms into lassitude, the improvement in mood and optimism begins to turn into procrastination, the need to do boring things, things one doesn't like to do but has to do, is put off until later ("I'll do it later...", "I'll do it tomorrow..."), the increase in creative ideas transforms into merely theoretical creativity ("Alright..."). And one becomes increasingly Ego/istic, more Ego/centric, more focused on "their" pleasure, "their" leisure, "their" happiness, just as the System likes...

Generally, the use of Cannabis begins in pre-adolescence or adolescence, and this can cause entry into the adult world to be delayed, and the young person remains young, and does not mature... and the years pass, and the young person does not mature... they remain forever young, only they are no longer so young, but their self-image is of a young person, and then the worst begins: they can become ridiculous. They dress like a teenager and they are already an adult, but they don't see themselves as an adult, they still feel like a young person, but they are no longer... Their old friends, "square," have become adults, and they haven't. Someone studied, made an effort, finished college, is working, earning money, doing things, and them? Still living and financially dependent on their parents, who then become blamed for their situation. Or, if not their parents, it's society, the world, politicians... Becoming an adult is nothing more than becoming a warrior of Light, serious, responsible, dynamic, engaged in the world, truly revolutionary in the sense of helping the world improve.

The daily use of this plant relaxed, calmed, internalized, but its goals and ideas were transformed into merely a mental journey, without practical realization. And two things can happen: a tendency towards isolation and loneliness, or an excess of socialization, an excess of intimacy, without criteria, with a loss of self-criticism. Depending on the user's personality, they may not perceive the evolutionary ruin of their physical appearance, gradually losing care for their clothes, their hair, their posture, and their attitudes, to the point where people see what they have become, less than themselves, because they go to places with the characteristic smell of the plant without realizing it, they think that putting on deodorant or perfume will disguise the smell, but it doesn't, or they sneak away, disappear, and then return with the characteristic childlike smile, talking nonsense, laughing for no reason, or hiding in corners.

Her posture, her speech, her conduct, begin to reveal that she is replacing her health, her maturity, her positive energy, her warrior side, with an egocentric, theoretical activity, of someone who refuses to truly mature, to become an adult, on a mental journey through spiritual worlds that will not benefit her or the people she lives with, nor people who would need her attention and care, such as the sick, the poor, the disabled, in an exercise of charity, care, and affection, which her heart asks for, but which would require an active, mature, proactive attitude, and not a passive, adolescent-like one, as the use of this plant can create and perpetuate.

The happiness that the person felt at the beginning of use turns into a childish joy, because the daily use of this plant can prevent the person from maturing; an 18-year-old behaves like a 14-year-old, or less, a person in their twenties looks like a teenager, in language, in the way they dress, in their posture, and this begins to reflect in their studies, in their work, and the use of a substance prohibited by law can create symptoms such as a persecution delusion, visions may appear, strange spiritual ideas may emerge, especially if the user begins to be (poorly) accompanied by disincarnated spirits, ex-users, who begin to influence their thoughts, their habits, until they completely dominate their mind and will.

Our society, still materialistic and dominated by the pastime and futility industry, subtly encourages the use of substances that make young people and people in general “happy” and lighter, such as alcohol and marijuana. No one smokes a joint and goes to work in a charity, goes to help someone in difficulty; they usually go to listen to music, have fun. The use of marijuana thus becomes a selfish, egocentric exercise, which aims only to make the user feel happy, even seeing so much unhappiness around them, even knowing that they could be using their time better for themselves, studying more, making more effort, working more, helping others, helping the world to improve more effectively. In the case of young people or even adults from lower social classes, its use serves to console them, to help them cope, to deal better with this condition, and no one can be against the use of something that meets this need. But its chronic, daily use can turn into accommodation, into moments of “happiness,” of relaxation, when they could use the same time to strive harder to get out of this condition that life has given them, to excel at school, at their work, to reverse an adverse situation into something much better. But the use of marijuana, as well as alcohol, crack, and others that are coming, always with the same purpose – “to bring joy” – can cause time, which could be used for progress, for the search for material, financial success, to be directed only to calm down, to relax.

In the case of young and middle or upper-class adults, the use of marijuana, alcoholic beverages, cocaine ("rich man's drug") is an exercise in selfishness, a testament to the misuse of time, directed only at oneself, and not at others, for one's "peace" and not for the most needy, the deprived, the sick in hospitals, daycares, nursing homes, on the streets.
Many Cannabis users are fans of Osho and his libertarian message. See the guidance Osho gave to a user of this plant:

A young man talks to the Master and tells him: “I smoke marijuana and when I smoke I know who I am. I feel God inside me, I see Him in everything. I talk to the grass, I talk to the flowers, and they answer. I feel happy, I feel completely content. But afterwards it gives me a pressure in my head that worries me, so I don't know if I should smoke or not, but it gives me great hope for the future. Smoking marijuana I have visions of where I would like to be. What do you think about it?”.

__QUOTE__This hope is merely illusory, it is not a real hope. The whole thing is just a chemical illusion and the chemical change may be giving you pressure in your head because the whole thing happens in your nervous system. That pressure is an indication to stop it. In time, it could be dangerous, as it could destroy some necessary nerves in the brain. It is destructive, it is a very expensive dream. It is beautiful, but even if a dream is beautiful it is a dream, and in the morning you are back again to reality, and it costs a lot. If you continue consuming marijuana for too long, it will make your intelligence deteriorate. People who smoke marijuana or things like that for too long become idiots. Their intelligence loses its sharpness because the daily chemical pressure on the nerves is harmful. You are gaining nothing, just an illusion. When you smoke marijuana and think you know who you are, that is of no importance whatsoever. You have to know that when you are alert, attentive, completely natural, without any pressure from chemical substances creating things in you. That is when you have to know who you are. We have to be enlightened in a common way, only then is it true enlightenment. We can find shortcuts, but all shortcuts are false. There is no shortcut to self-realization. Shortcuts only create small circuits within you and release dreams, release imagination. That is not good for you, it is not good for anyone. It is better to stop as soon as possible. Creating an experience that is not your natural state is useless. It destroys your life and destroys your opportunities to become alert, aware of reality as it is. You don't need to seek God in trees. If you can just see trees as they are, everything is perceived. Why impose God? You don't need to see God in anyone and in anything. If you can just see the real person who is there, that is enough! God simply means reality, He is the common reality that surrounds you. When I say that God is in the trees, I don't mean that you will have to see God in the trees, that a head will start to blossom on the tree, then someone will look at you and you will have a meeting and a dialogue and that someone will say “Hi!”. When I say “See God in the trees,” I simply mean to see the tree as it is without any idea on your part. See the truth of the tree. That is the God of the tree, its greenness, its flower, its joy, its rooting, its strength and fragility. How can you see God in the tree? A tree is a tree! You should not be deceived, but when you smoke marijuana you become a fool, and then it is very easy to be deceived. To see reality, one has to be completely common and not use anything, no fasting, no postures, one just has to be as they are. Therefore, do not rush and do not run. Yes, drugs give speed, but do not speed up and do not rush artificially. Be patient and allow things to grow slowly. All real things grow slowly, they have their own rhythm. What you told me means that something has to ripen in you. Be satisfied and content with whatever is available at this very moment, ask for no more. I know that using any drug, this becomes very difficult because the drug attracts. Without any effort on your part, something starts to happen, so why bother with anything else? Why meditate and why be alert when the drug can trigger the process immediately? It has been used for centuries, it is nothing new. In the West it is a new thing, but in the East it is one of the oldest practices. But people who take drugs never get anywhere. If you really want to see what is inside you, you have to stop all types of projection. It will seem silly at first. It will not be so charming, it will not have so much attraction, so much fascination, but fascination, enchantment, that is not necessary. One should be satisfied with common reality. If you can do this for six months without the drug, living only with the common, without desiring the extraordinary, you will begin to see the truth of common things. And in the common itself the extraordinary is hidden. The ordinary is the door to the extraordinary. My suggestion is that you drop it, stop completely!||Osho

The beginning of using this plant is a honeymoon, but after this honeymoon, over time, discomfort often begins to arise when without it, the need for its effect starts to increase, one has to smoke otherwise one feels bad, boredom comes, irritation, anxiety, what is called tolerance begins, which is the need for more Cannabis for the same effect of feeling peace, of feeling good, and dependence begins, which is the difficulty of controlling consumption. Everyone says they could stop, that they are not addicted, but they don't stop… They are addicted to feeling artificially good, to feeling peace from the outside in. And how to free oneself from this? In general, Cannabis users despise all scientific information that warns about the dangers of use, thinking that this is being square, they disregard what adults say, their parents, doctors, scientists, believing that they are “squares,” “don't know anything,” when in reality they are concerned about them, often trying to prevent them from doing what they themselves did wrong in their youth.

What leads a young person to use substances is usually the search for pleasure, joy, and excitement, but this can end up affecting the sense of reality of life and making them navigate a sea of only dreams and fantasies, without concretization, without a positive result, without practice. Often the beginning of its use was an attempt to alleviate feelings of loneliness, inadequacy, low self-esteem, or lack of confidence. Often its use is a way of asserting oneself as equal within one's group, as there are rules in the group that are accepted and valued by its members, such as the use of certain clothes, hairstyles, language, frequency to certain places, and the use of drugs. So one has to be the same, one cannot be different, one cannot be square… It is in the group that the young person seeks their identity, but the young person has free will in choosing their group of companions. In fact, the type of group with which they identify has to do with their personality and their tendencies.

A strong motivation for a young person to seek substances is transgression, and to transgress is to contest, to be against the family, against society and its values. A certain dose of transgression in adolescence is even normal and beneficial, but when it exceeds a normal limit, it represents disillusionment and disenchantment, and the danger becomes the generalization and trivialization of life, like “No one is good…”, “They're all a bunch of jerks”, “So-and-so didn't study and did well in life…”, “Whoever studies is square…” etc.

Young people often use certain substances to point out the incoherence of the adult world that uses and abuses very harmful, though legalized, drugs such as alcohol, cigarettes, and psychotropic medications. Youthful omnipotence is a characteristic of adolescence that makes young people believe that nothing bad will happen. They can have unprotected sex and won't get pregnant, they will never get AIDS or another STD, they can use drugs and won't become dependent. And the risk of dependence in young people is even greater when they have difficulty dealing with authority figures, because then they compulsively challenge and transgress, and can self-destruct to prove they are right…

Adolescents are influenced by fads and subcultures, they are rebellious, they suffer conflicts between dependence and independence, they want to be independent without being financially independent, they have a strong group consciousness and feel, rightly so, a displeasure with routinized urban life and often end up taking wrong paths to escape this routine. In the 60s, many people began to question social reality and seek psychic healing in nature, as the urban world seemed to offer no alternatives. They learned to use certain plants to modify conscious perception; it was the era of hippies. Today, 30 years later, most have recognized their mistake, as they saw that this false divine sensation ended up anesthetizing their individual reality of not feeling “good enough,” and realized that being “square” brings more peace, more direction, less selfishness of traveling within oneself, forgetting charity, helping others, caring for the poor, the sick, the most needy. fobias: Phobia originates from the Greek Phobia, meaning intense or irrational fear, aversion, hostility. According to the official psychiatric paradigm, it is an "excessive and unreal fear" of an object, person, animal, activity, or situation. It is a type of anxiety disorder.
__SUB__The traditional view

Some phobias are specific, for example, a person may only be afraid of spiders (Arachnophobia) or cats (Galeophobia). Some phobias cause difficulty in a wider variety of places or situations, for example, the fear of heights (Acrophobia) simply by looking out of an apartment window, driving over a high bridge, and even seeing scenes of height in movies or on television. The fear of enclosed spaces (Claustrophobia) can be activated when entering an elevator, an enclosed room, etc.

People with these phobias generally need to alter their lives drastically, and in extreme cases, a phobia can dictate a person's profession, their workplace, their driving route, their social activities, or their home environment. According to the World Health Organization, 15% of the world's population suffers from some type of Phobia.
__SUB__The reincarnationist view

Excessive and unreal fear: For people suffering from the symptoms of phobias, it is horrible and very traumatic to call their symptoms "excessive and unreal." It's like the cases of people who hear voices and see beings that traditional Psychiatry says don't exist, that are unreal. How so, unreal? Some people see and hear voices of Spirits and others don't. Do the voices many people hear and the beings they claim to see exist or not? Psychiatry states that they don't exist, it's a disease, it's schizophrenia, it requires medication, but Catholicism, Evangelical Churches, Spiritism, Umbanda, Judaism, affirm that they do exist, they are Spirits. Psychiatrists are usually from one of these Religions, so why, when in their office, do they not believe that some people may have the ability to see or hear voices of discarnate Spirits?

In the case of the "excessive and unreal fears" of phobias, they are neither excessive nor unreal; they are genuinely strong and intense because, at the moment the person is experiencing the symptoms, they are much more in a past life than here, in the present moment. When a person has a Phobia of enclosed spaces and panics inside an elevator, for example, they start getting nervous, sweating, wanting to get out at all costs, starting to scream and bang on the walls. Someone might tell them: "Fulano, it's just an elevator, calm down!" It's no use, they are not in the elevator at that moment; they are in a hole in a past life where they were trapped for a long time, they are inside the coffin where their dead body was placed and they remained (as a Spirit) for a long time, they are in a collapse or burial where they died, they are in a prison where they are about to be executed—in short, they are panicking in an enclosed space in another incarnation!

And it can be disconnected from that situation through Inquiry into the Unconscious, through Meditation, by accessing the situation, recalling it until its end, until everything has passed and they are well; this way, that situation never bothers them again. Generally, there are 4 or 5 past situations (from past lives, from the current intrauterine period, or early childhood) responsible for the symptoms, and the person needs to disconnect from all of them, one by one. With each one they disconnect from, they already feel better; by disconnecting from all, the Phobia ends. Within the Unconscious, there is no time, everything is present, and when a past situation becomes active, it is happening. The person with Phobia doesn't know why they panicked, but the fear they feel is not unreal or excessive; it is real and genuinely intense.

Normally, we are all tuned into situations from past lives. In the case of "phobic" patients, they are tuned into some old situations that remain within their Unconscious; they remain inactive when they are not facing something similar to what happened in the past, but when they encounter a situation that resonates within them, the person undergoes a spontaneous Regression and "goes there," that is, at that moment it's as if they are 80% or more there and 20% or less here, and they panic! And then they say that what they feel is "excessive and unreal." What do we do? The person, lying down, conscious, lucid, awake, in meditation, after 15 to 20 minutes, accesses their past, begins to recall and tell us about that situation; we encourage them to recall it to the end to disconnect from it. In addition to the person disconnecting from pathogenic situations, they begin to understand that they were not sick, nor fussy, nor exaggerated, nor manipulative; they were tuned into one or more past life situations that traumatized them and to which they returned when they encountered a similar situation today. We do not use medications; they are not necessary; if the person is taking medications, we do not interfere; they continue taking them, but after disconnecting from the situations they were tuned into, they are cured, then they can talk to their psychiatrist to gradually reduce until completely withdrawn.

Arachnophobia: I ask: when someone claims to have a Phobia of enclosed spaces, where are they tuned in their past? In an enclosed space where something highly traumatic happened or they were forcibly placed and stayed there for a long time or even died, etc. So, when someone claims to have a Phobia of spiders, where are they tuned in? In a situation in another incarnation where there were many spiders, like a hole in the ground, in a cave, etc. And nowadays, when they are not exposed to the sight of a spider, they are 100% in this current life, but when a spider appears, they spontaneously regress to that place in their past where the spiders were and, as they were in a panic there because of them, they panic now! And they are told that their fear is excessive and unreal, and they are treated for years because of it, with medications, suffering, anguish, distrust, and irony from other people, when they would only need to find the situation(s) that originated this Phobia, remember, disconnect from it(them), and their problem is solved.

Galeophobia: We treat few people with Phobia of cats, but all of them accessed situations from other incarnations where they were attacked by lions, tigers, or similar animals. Nowadays, they see a simple kitten, but their Unconscious sees that enormous animal and they panic!

Acrophobia: Let's do a test: When a person suffers from Phobia of high places, what kind of situation are they tuned into in their past? 1) In a low place. 2) In a high place. The correct answer is 2, in a high place, and in that incarnation they fell (or jumped or were thrown) from a mountain, their plane was shot down in the war, they committed suicide by throwing themselves from a bridge, etc. And nowadays, when they are in a high place, look out of an apartment window, see a scene of height on TV, what happens? They undergo a spontaneous Regression and go back to that situation in their past, and they panic.

Claustrophobia: Let's see if you're a good student. When someone feels a lot of fear in an enclosed space and panics, what kind of situation from another incarnation are they still tuned into? You got it right if you said "enclosed space." Very good, congratulations, that's exactly it.
__SUB__Types of Phobias
__SUB__Traditional view

Specific Phobia (Simple Phobia): This is the most common form of Phobia. In this type, people may fear certain "animals" (dogs, cats, spiders, snakes, etc.), certain people (clowns, dentists, doctors, etc.), environments ("dark places," "storms," "high places," etc.), or situations (traveling by plane, confined spaces like tunnels, traffic jams, etc.), seeing "blood," driving, etc. The non-reincarnationist paradigm of traditional Psychiatry states that although the cause of specific Phobias remains a "mystery," these conditions are partly "genetic" (inherited) and appear to occur in other family members. Other Specific Phobias: Hydrophobia, which consists of an intense fear of water, "the fear of drowning," Hypochondria ("Phobia of illness") where there is a dread of getting sick; they go from doctor to doctor, and all of them give a negative diagnosis regarding a physical illness. The diagnosis is that their ailment is psychological; they do not accept this and continue consulting in the hope that some doctor will find the cause of their symptoms, but they don't, and they become disillusioned because they feel sick, have symptoms, but nothing appears on clinical or laboratory exams, even the most sophisticated.

Social Phobia: In this type of Phobia, people are afraid of social situations where they "might be humiliated or judged by others" or a fear of "embarrassing themselves" in front of others. The fear can be of public speaking, speaking in class (usually starts in childhood), giving a lecture, presenting a paper, taking an oral exam, etc. They may start to avoid social situations, such as going to parties, eating in public, or using a public restroom. Social Phobia 'appears in other members of the same family.' People who were 'shy in childhood,' who have a history of unhappy or 'negative social experiences in childhood,' seem to have a 'propensity to develop this disorder.' Another example: 'signing checks or writing in front of others,' talking to 'authority figures,' etc.

Agoraphobia: It is a fear of being in public places where it would be difficult and embarrassing to leave suddenly or where help might not be available. This includes being alone outside the house, in crowds, in a queue, traveling alone, etc. A person with Agoraphobia may avoid going to a cinema or a concert, traveling on a bus or a train. Agoraphobia, literally, is the fear of the Agora (the "market squares" where people were hanged in public sessions).
__SUB__The reincarnationist view

"Animal Phobia": In practically all cases of animal Phobia, in Regressions, people encountered an attack by an animal in past incarnations, a ferocious animal, tiger, wolf, etc. This is already registered within the person's Unconscious in such a way that, generally, from childhood they are afraid of dogs, cats, and can panic when they encounter one of these animals, even a small one or a puppy! At this moment of "excessive and unreal" fear, the person has regressed to the situation of the past life where they are on the verge of or being attacked by a truly ferocious animal! In other words, today's kitten is yesterday's tiger, the puppy is the wolf there...

"Phobia of certain people": Imagine a child who is very afraid of clowns. Their father, their mother, play with them, want them to approach, touch the clown, but they panic, cry, want to be held, want to run away, people laugh, say it's just a child's thing. That night they don't sleep well, the father or mother has to sleep with them, holding hands, they are very agitated, when they manage to sleep they have nightmares, scream, wake up, in short it's a terrible night. From the next day on, they are strange, with dark circles under their eyes, don't want to go to school, don't want to leave the house, say they are afraid, the clown might be outside, the parents get angry, lose patience, even threaten to spank them if they don't stop with that nonsense... Nonsense? This is the beginning of the ordeal of millions of people who, as adults, suffering from Phobia or Panic Disorder or Depression, claim that it started in their childhood, sometimes remembering the situation that originated it. It didn't start in childhood, if it had started there it wouldn't be so strong and lasting, it started in a previous incarnation when someone in showy, flashy clothes, some black magic ritual, a human sacrifice, something like that, happened and this was inactive within their Unconscious, until the sight of the clown made the situation activate and a spontaneous Regression occurs and the Phobia begins. And a Phobia of the dentist or doctor? Sitting, at someone's mercy, on the verge of feeling pain, with little or no possibility of defending oneself, of facing, what must have happened in another era similar to this? Torture? A judgment?

"Phobia of dark places": In all Regressions with people having this Phobia, they accessed one or more past incarnations, the current womb, or a situation in their childhood, where they were in a dark place, with great fear or in a panic. They recalled the situation, its ending, until they were well, and healed the symptoms of their connection to that situation. In the case of Phobia of dark places, in some incarnations, they experienced this situation and need to recall all of them, one by one, until the end of the situation, until the trauma in those occasions has passed. It is as if the Unconscious needs to remember that it is over, and then the Phobia disappears.

"Phobia of storms": This Phobia comes from situations in other incarnations where the person was in a storm and a flood, a fire, they lost their home, or they died, or a family member, in short, an extreme situation where something very serious happened. When in the current life the weather starts to close in, it threatens to rain or lightning and thunder begin, they undergo a spontaneous Regression to that situation and panic!

"Phobia of high places": They were in a high place in a past life and fell, or were thrown, or saw this happen to a friend or relative. When today they go to their apartment window, are in an elevator, or see a scene of height on TV, they regress, regress, until they reach the original situation, and they panic! Are you finding all this very repetitive and rational? Phobia of storms, the cause was a storm; phobia of high places, the cause was a high place... What can I do? That's how it is. Why would someone have a Phobia of something that hasn't really happened? But the psychiatric paradigm is non-reincarnationist, although 2/3 of humanity believes in Reincarnation, and many psychiatrists are spiritists, Buddhists, Umbandistas, Jews, etc., but not in their practice.

"Phobia of blood": These cases of Phobia come from situations where the person died with a lot of bleeding, a wound from a spear, sword, in a battle, in a war, an accident, a surgery, something like that. And this was imprinted in their memory so that, today, when they see a scene of blood on TV, or have to go to a hospital, or get injured and bleed, or see someone bleeding, they undergo a Regression and go back there...

A person came for consultation due to great anxiety, unable to see blood, enter a hospital, draw blood, have exams, felt a sensation that something very bad could happen. He himself, sometimes, considered himself a failure… See below his case with his own testimony, where it came from and how he is today. Here is his report after Regression and significant improvement:

__QUOTE__In mid-April 2007, I decided to seek Regression Therapy. I had never participated in a similar experience, but I believed, as I still believe, that a large part of our problems originate from events that occurred in the successive lives we have had. What made me seek this therapy was the need to resolve a kind of Phobia I possessed. I had great difficulty relaxing physically, always apprehensive, as if something bad was always imminent, some accident, some injury to myself or another person. A fear of having to experience something traumatic involving blood. It was a constant state of alert. In the first Regression Session, I saw myself as a man, being chased, with a young woman and a little girl, us running through the streets hand in hand. It seemed to be a place where there was a revolt in the streets, an armed conflict, perhaps a battle, a war, a lot of agitation. At a certain moment, I am shot in the back and fall. The young woman accompanying me approached where I had fallen, caressed my head, crying. At that moment I also hear the girl's crying, who is taken by other people amidst the confusion. I have the impression that blood is flowing from my mouth, I feel a lot of pain in my back, I am dying. I leave my body, in the Spiritual World, I receive treatment, a fluidification in the place in my back where I was shot. It is a calm, peaceful place. When I arrive, I am received by someone who seems to be my deceased grandmother (current). Nowadays, I have a back problem that, according to doctors, would require surgical intervention, but since the Regressions I have had a significant improvement, as if the discovery of the possible perispiritual mark caused a relief in the current physical symptoms (Note: and the disconnection from that situation). Today I can face situations that were once quite conflicting with serenity and courage and, day by day, I try to reaffirm new purposes, new convictions, seeking to correct emotional patterns that induce me to wrong behavior patterns.||Patient's testimony

"Mystery": For someone like me, who has already heard about the past lives of some 20,000 people, it is no mystery. It seems a mystery to mental and emotional health professionals who do not yet deal with Reincarnation and to people who "do not believe" in it. I put "do not believe" in quotes, because this is not the result of an intense study of Reincarnation, of a broad and deep research; it is simply an opinion. I believe because I have studied it deeply for over 20 years. So I can say that the origin of phobias is no mystery.
"Genetic": It's not genetic, it's pre-genetic, it comes in our psychobioenergetic mold.

"Fear of drowning": Why would someone be afraid of drowning if they haven't drowned, or almost, before? A little fear, not liking to take risks, not going to the deep end of the sea, where you can't touch the bottom, to the deep end of the pool, I myself don't like it and don't go, but to have a Phobia of water, to panic? Only if you have already drowned, or almost, in a past life, or in the womb, or in current childhood, and this is what appears in the Regressions. For example: a person with a Phobia of water, accessed a past incarnation where they were on a boat that capsized and they drowned, then discarnated, remembered that they ascended to the Spiritual World, got well, everything passed. When they reincarnated, that trauma came within their Unconscious, so that, when they are not in water or near water, they don't feel this fear, but if they travel by ship, enter a pool or the sea, or even watch a movie or a documentary on TV that has water, that traumatic situation, hidden within their Unconscious, begins to activate and they regress to that ship, they are falling into the water, they are drowning, they are going to die, they panic! Sometimes people ask me if all Phobias come from past lives, if they couldn't have started in this current life. A fear of something can have its origin in the current life, a Phobia cannot; what can happen is a reinforcement within the womb, at the time of birth, in the first years out here or later, but if the person does not come with a similar highly traumatic situation from another incarnation, what happens today can cause a little fear, a little discomfort, but a Phobia, Panic, severe Depression, only occurs if they have already reincarnated with it.

"Hypochondria": Some people who suffered, in past lives, from serious diseases, of long duration, with much suffering, come to this life with this information in their Unconscious, and any little pain, twinge, fever, unpleasant sensation, resonates there in their past and that situation becomes active! Here they have a twinge in their chest, there they died of a long and endless illness. One day, this person starts to feel abdominal pains, goes to the doctor, has clinical, laboratory, radiological exams, all show that they have nothing, but they feel the pain, they don't accept it, there must be something, they feel it. What is it? In a past life, they died with a spear stuck in their belly. The diagnosis is Hypochondria, but it isn't, they are tuned into a traumatic situation from a past incarnation. There are people who believe they will die of a heart attack; in a past life, they died suddenly like that, with intense suffering, hours and hours of anguish, of pain. In other people it is the dread of having cancer, of having AIDS, always serious, dangerous diseases, life threats; we help them to go back to their past, see where this comes from and disconnect from the situations.

"Fear of being humiliated or judged by others": In my experience, people who feel this fear have indeed been humiliated and judged in past lives. This may have been in a court in another life, a family or group judgment, etc. In 22 years of work, I have never seen a person claim to be afraid of being humiliated or judged who had not actually been so, in another life. Sometimes, the person had a very critical, judgmental father or mother, who demanded too much from them, only criticized them, did not praise them, only pointed out errors, mistakes, and then it seems that their Phobia of being humiliated, judged, came from childhood, from that "villain" father, that "villain" mother, and the psychological treatment is addressed to this, to understand their father, their mother; after all, they also had strict parents, it's what they learned, no one gives more than they can, etc. And so the years pass, the various psychotherapies, trying to forgive their critical father, their judgmental mother, until one day they undergo some Regression Sessions and discover that their fear comes from a past life in which they were judged and humiliated in court. Years and years of therapy to forgive their parents, who are now old, or even deceased, and who were not even the "villains," unless one or both of them were on that jury, but this is hidden within their Unconscious and since we, from the Brazilian Association of Reincarnationist Psychotherapy (ABPR), work with Ethical Regression, in which we never encourage recognition during the regressive process, thus managing to reconcile Regression Therapy with the Law of Forgetfulness, very rarely does anyone recognize someone during a Regression.

"Fear of embarrassment": People who have this fear have often been embarrassed in a past life; one was a domestic worker feeling ridiculed by the rich, another was a beggar scorned by people, another a servant treated as an inferior by nobles, etc. They may also have done something in a past life and were ridiculed, laughed at, and today they are afraid to speak in public; they are that quiet child in class, who doesn't speak, who turns red when they have to stand up, when they have to speak; then others laugh, ridicule them, they suffer from bullying, and the more this happens, the more the action of that past life increases within their Unconscious, the more they close themselves off, become silent. One day they no longer want to leave home, no longer want to socialize with people, saying that everyone just wants to humiliate them, just wants to offend them... Regressions in children can be done on them or on their mother or another family member (Distance Regression - RAD -, in which, if authorized by the child's Spiritual Mentors, the person can access the life in which they are attuned, recall it until the end of that life, disconnect them from there, and the improvement is very great and very fast). We have about 40 free RAD Groups on the ABPR Portal.

"Social Phobia usually appears in other members of the same family": What is a family? It is a grouping of close incarnated Spirits, linked by energetic cords of affinity or divergence. Sometimes, some people from the same family present the same Phobia, and upon undergoing Regression, they discover that they were in that boat, that storm, that war, that earthquake, that attack, etc., that is, they went through the same situation in a past life.

"Timidity in childhood": The basic pillar of Reincarnationist Psychotherapy is Congenital Personality, which states that "We are as we are because we were born this way." Is there a simpler concept than this? And we are this way because in the previous incarnation we were already this way and, frequently, we have been this way for centuries, for many incarnations. Why is one child timid and their sibling extroverted? Why is one child fearful and another bold, intrepid? It would suffice for Psychology and Psychiatry to free themselves from that decision of the Second Council of Constantinople in 553 AD and adhere to the new, reincarnationist paradigm. They say that intense shyness is genetic, it's environmental, it's mysterious – no, it's the Congenital Personality that André Luiz talks about in "Obreiros da Vida Eterna" (Workers of Eternal Life), psychographed by Chico Xavier, published in 1946. And in Congenital Personality lies our proposal for Inner Reform. Let's do a test: In your opinion, what was a very timid child like in their past life before this one? Timid. And why were they timid in the past? What happened that made them like that? Well, then we need to do some Regression Sessions. Why is a child fearful? They were born fearful. And a child who is easily hurt, always feels rejected? They were born hurt. And a child who thinks they are better than others, is vain, wants to be rich, famous? They were born vain. And another who thinks less of themselves than others, inferior? They were born thinking less of themselves. The answer lies in Congenital Personality, the basis of Reincarnationist Psychotherapy. And there lies the proposal for Inner Reform. It is enough to understand this to know our proposal for evolution in this current incarnation.

"Propensity to develop this disorder": How to understand this without adding Reincarnation to the reasoning in search of a diagnosis? This propensity comes from past lives; the current incarnation is merely the continuation of previous lives. What we remember is called the Conscious; what we don't remember is called the Unconscious. The origin of inexplicable fears, of "motiveless" panic, of severe depressions resistant to treatment, of chronic physical pains without a defined diagnosis (like Fibromyalgia, for example, easily cured with a few Regression Sessions), of autoimmune diseases, etc., are hidden within people's Unconscious, which Dr. Freud instructed to open and investigate, but his disciples did not follow the Viennese Master; we are his followers.

"Signing checks or writing in front of others": In all cases of people with this complaint, they encountered old situations in which they signed documents, a decree, a petition, etc., and this caused a disastrous and unpleasant consequence, something that resulted in moral or financial harm, their imprisonment, and even their death. In that incarnation where this happened, they knew what was happening, the cause of it all; nowadays, when they are going to sign a check, a document, a power of attorney, they undergo a spontaneous Regression and go back there, and start to panic, tremble, don't want to sign, but people are around, waiting for them to sign, they are here and there. This is called a Phobia of signing checks or writing in front of others, and like other Phobias, it can be treated by going to the origin.

"Authority figures": Let's say you are a person who suffers from a Phobia of speaking to someone who represents an authority figure to you. This has been happening since you were a child, when you had to speak to your teacher, stand up in class to answer a question, when you already showed fear of a police officer, of people who came to your house, etc. You grew up, this fear was worked on, it's better, but when you have to go into your boss's office, your heart starts racing, when you go to knock on the door, you want to give up, if he tells you to come in, your heart seems to come out of your mouth, you enter trembling, you can't speak, he seems like a monster! What is this? Why do you feel this way? It's that at that moment, for example, you are in another incarnation before a Judge in a court, you are going to be judged for something you did or that is attributed to you, and probably on that occasion it resulted in your hanging, guillotine, a very long prison sentence or for life, etc. In the current incarnation, you always believed that this was due to the fact that your father in childhood was very strict, or your mother was very authoritarian, and now you find the origin of your Phobia in another incarnation, you recall, disconnect from that situation, the improvement is fast. So many years blaming your father, your mother…

"Agoraphobia": It's interesting that the name for the Phobia of large, open places is Agoraphobia, with Agora being the name given to market squares in ancient times. A large part of Agoraphobia cases have their origin in judgments, hangings, guillotines, traumatic situations that happened when the person was in one of these squares! In those days, when there was no television, this type of event took place in a square and the defendants, those condemned to death, were executed there, in the presence of the people. Imagine the trauma of being executed there, on a kind of stage, under the gaze of all those people and, probably, some shouting insults, laughing, mocking, and some friends and family suffering, crying... All of this remains within their Unconscious and, today, when they are in a large, open place, in a crowd (cinema, soccer game, concert, shopping mall), they experience a spontaneous Regression at that moment and are no longer just in the current life, they are here and there, in their execution or on the verge of being killed. They panic, as they did there, and want to run away, escape, go home, find a way out quickly, feel short of breath, their heart races, they start to sweat. Someone might tell them: "Oh, so-and-so, relax, there's no danger, we're at the movies, or at a concert, nothing can happen!" But the poor person is not at the movies or the concert, they are there in that square and they are going to die! They need to be disconnected from there.
__SUB__Symptoms of Phobia
__SUB__Traditional view
Psychiatry states that people with Phobias present characteristic symptoms that can be classified as:

__LIST__Excessive, "irrational" and persistent feelings of fear or anxiety that are triggered by a particular object, activity or situation: The feelings are "out of proportion" to any actual threat. For example: anyone can be afraid of being threatened by an unrestrained dog, but it is not reasonable to run from a calm, quiet animal on a leash.||Physical symptoms related to anxiety: These symptoms may include "tremors, palpitations, sweating, shortness of breath, dizziness, nausea, or other symptoms that reflect a 'fight or flight' danger response."||Avoidance of the object, activity, or situation that triggers the Phobia: "As people with Phobia recognize that their fears are irrational, they often feel ashamed or embarrassed about their symptoms." To prevent anxiety or embarrassment symptoms, they avoid the Phobia triggers, which often radically changes their lives.
__SUB__Reincarnationist view

1. Irrational: We have already seen that the symptoms are not without reason; people who report a Phobia of something have reason to feel this terrible fear in a situation, because one died drowned in a past life and is afraid to enter a pool; another was hanged in a public square and is afraid to be in a crowd at a concert; a person died burned at the stake during the Inquisition and today is afraid that their house will catch fire, checking the stove several times; in short, every Phobia has an origin in an extremely traumatic event from another incarnation and its symptoms are real and coherent.

2. Out of proportion: The extreme fear that a phobic person feels in the face of a triggering situation is not out of proportion; it is equal to the terror they felt in the original situation of their Phobia. A person suffering from Phobia is generally attuned to four, five, or more past incarnations, and through treatment with Inquiry into the Unconscious, they are disconnected from them, one by one. The person becomes free of these symptoms and learns where their fear and anxiety came from. Imagine the comfort this represents for someone who is considered a failure, a sick person, an exaggerator, someone who "wants attention." It is none of that; these are traumas from other incarnations that their Unconscious wants to release, and Regressions make this possible. Phobia is not a disease; it is a circumstance.

3. Tremors, palpitations, sweating, shortness of breath, dizziness, nausea, or other symptoms reflecting a "fight or flight" danger response: These symptoms arise from the increase in Adrenaline that occurs when the person enters a Phobia crisis, as the panic they enter causes this hormone to increase, and this causes these symptoms, which further increase the panic! To surrounding people, it seems like nonsense, a hysterical attack, a scandal, but to the person, that fear is terrible; it is a real and true anguishing sensation. Adrenaline is a hormone that increases when we face situations where we have to confront something, fight, defend ourselves, or flee, and this is exactly what happens in a panic attack in a Phobia, but it was usually something very traumatic for oneself, in which one suffered pain, a tragic death, etc.

4. As people with Phobia recognize that their fears are irrational, they often feel ashamed or embarrassed about their symptoms: When the symptoms started, they didn't think they were "irrational" or "exaggerated"; it was non-reincarnationist professionals who told them or insinuated that it was "all in their head," "that it was nothing," or that they were "irrational discharges of their neurotransmitters"; it was their friends and family who thought and still think that, and they, then, began to feel ashamed and embarrassed for feeling this "irrational," "causeless," "out of proportion" fear. I have never had a Phobia, but I have been treating people with Phobia for many years and I know how unpleasant it is to have a terrible fear of entering an elevator, of leaving home, of being in a high place, in an open place, in front of animals, and being seen as an exaggerator, a manipulator, a sick person who has this fear for no reason, being treated as a psychiatric case. No one understands them, not even themselves. Anyone who comes to my office with a Phobia, I tell them that we should look within their Unconscious for the situation that originated their fear, whether from a past life, or from the womb, or from the first years of life, but I never tell them that their fear is irrational, is disproportionate, because I know that their fear is rational and proportional to the intensity of what they felt in the original situation.
(extracted from the book "Treating Phobia, Panic and Depression with Past Life Regression Therapy" – Mauro Kwitko – Besourobox Edições)
